races.race_name: Arizona Blaze Standing At The Irish National Stud Irish EBF Race

runners.name: Belle Island
runners.spotlight: One of three for trainer; both runs came over 5f at Dundalk; beaten a shade over 4l on debut and improved to finish a 1.75l third last time; others preferred.
runners.comment: Third of six on second m'den run at Dundalk last time; others preferred
runners.owner: Gary Devlin & N W T Dalzell

runners.name: Carry The Flag
runners.spotlight: No Nay Never colt; 250,000euros yearling; half-brother to winners Raafedd (7f; RPR 100), Secret Hideaway (5f 2yo Listed; 88) and Elsals (7f; 87); dam Listed-placed 5f 2yo winner (90), half-sister to 1m2f winner Aunt Bee, out of 7f 2yo Group 3 winner; interesting on paper and should be competitive on debut.
runners.comment: No Nay Never colt was a 250,000euros yearling; should be competitive on debut

runners.name: What A Girl Wants
runners.spotlight: One of three for trainer; 90,000gns yearling; half-sister to winners Dubai Dominion (7f-8.5f inc 2yo/Australia; RPR 102), Evening Saigon (5f turf 2yo/6f AW; 95), Peace Walk (7.5f 2yo; 83) and Hit Mac (5f AW; 67); dam 6f AW winner (inc 2yo; 107), out of 1m Listed winner; promising pedigree but David Egan rides Blixen Force.
runners.comment: Half-sister to four winners inc' Dubai Dominion (RPR 102); Egan rides Blixen Force

runners.name: Arizona Raider
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to winners Lady Chartwell (1m2f AW; RPR 84) and Komel (Italian 8.7f-1m7f inc 2yo); dam unraced half-sister to UAE 5f/6f Listed winner Colour Up, out of unraced half-sister to 5f Group 3 winner Land Of Dreams; there are easier races to start off in.
runners.comment: Half-brother to two winners inc' Lady Chartwell (RPR 84); there are easier races to start

runners.name: Star Prospect
runners.spotlight: Starman colt; 135,000euros yearling; half-brother to winners Ameeq (5f 2yo; RPR 94), Tell William (1m/8.5f inc Italy; 83), Wrought Iron (6f AW; 73) and Continental (6f AW; 66); dam 1m AW winner (79), half-sister to sprint Group 3 winners Swiss Spirit and Swiss Diva; deserving of a look in the market.
runners.comment: Starman colt is a half-brother to four winners; check market.

runners.name: Blixen Force
runners.spotlight: One of three for trainer; 30,000euros foal, 110,000GBP yearling; beaten favourite when third in the Brocklesby (5f, good to soft); hung right when challenging but promising run all the same; experience could be decisive here and he looks like stable number one.
runners.comment: Beaten fav' in Brocklesby when third; can put experience to use and Egan rides

races.race_name: SIS Supporting Irish Racing Handicap

runners.name: Buddy Batt
runners.spotlight: Three wins from four winter starts at Dundalk; soft going might not be ideal for his return to turf on a mark 16lb higher than when fifth of 16 in a C&D apprentice handicap last October.
runners.comment: Three wins from four winter starts at Dundalk, going might not be ideal for return to turf

runners.name: Cuban Grey
runners.spotlight: Won this race last year after a Bellewstown second and now bids to follow the same pattern after a good run eight days ago; fair chance that he can reverse Dundalk form with Buddy Batt.
runners.comment: Won this race last year after a Bellewstown second and now bids to follow the same pattern

runners.name: Noble Nation
runners.spotlight: Hacked up in an early-season 6f Naas event last year on soft-to-heavy, earning a 15lb hike in the ratings; lost form subsequently; was back on a reasonable mark for his reappearance in the same Naas contest this year, and took full advantage; has got away with a 4lb rise this time; form boosted by runner-up Exceeding at Leopardstown on Wednesday.
runners.comment: Repeated a 2025 Naas win four weeks ago, needs to sustain his form better than last season

runners.name: Likedbymike
runners.spotlight: Won over this trip at two; placed three times last year, including third behind today's rival Stag Night at Naas; may improve from a recent 6f run at this venue.
runners.comment: Won over this trip at two, placed three times last year, may improve from a recent 6f run
runners.owner: Mrs Michael J Byrne & Jerry P Murphy

runners.name: Ribble Emperor
runners.spotlight: Consistent on AW; has Dundalk form linked to recent winner Diego El Queso; however, that was over 1m, and he is an unknown quantity at this trip.
runners.comment: Has 1m Dundalk form linked to recent winner Diego El Queso, first attempt at this trip

runners.name: Stag Night
runners.spotlight: One win and five places from ten Curragh starts; good chance now after second over C&D last month, marking an improvement from a third placing over 6f on seasonal debut; best suited by soft ground.
runners.comment: One win and five places from ten Curragh starts, good chance now after second last month

runners.name: Gazelle d'Or
runners.spotlight: Productive campaign last year with three course wins, including over 6f on seasonal return; highlights were two C&D premier handicap wins, notably the Joe McGrath off 83 on yielding-to-soft; now rated 89, down in class again after a Listed attempt on final start.
runners.comment: Daul C&D premier handicap winner last season, down in class again after a Listed attempt

runners.name: Sarahmae
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner at 5f on good and yielding ground, including at this venue last August; has struggled in two 6f outings at this venue this term; probably needs the ground to improve.
runners.comment: C&D winner last August, has struggled in two 6f outings at this venue this term
runners.owner: Mary B Hogan & Michael M O'Brien

runners.name: Shadow Of The Moon
runners.spotlight: Opening mark of 80 is based on an emphatic 5f maiden at Down Royal on her third and final start at three; hard to be sure how she will fare here on different ground against more experienced opponents.
runners.comment: Opening mark of 80 is based on an emphatic 5f maiden at Down Royal, different ground here

runners.name: Sommelier
runners.spotlight: Smart juvenile form on AW; got to within 1.5l of Big Evs in a 3yo Listed race at York (5f, good) in May 2024 when trained by Marco Botti; lost his way and sold up for 26,000gns last June; 50-1 when in rear over C&D on Irish debut last month
runners.comment: Best form in Britain on AW, big price when in rear over C&D on Irish debut last month

races.race_name: Visit The Irish National Stud And Gardens Irish EBF Maiden

runners.name: Bull Shark
runners.spotlight: One of two for trainer; 120,000GBP yearling purchase; Mehmas colt shaped as though he would improve for the experience when third over 5f here last month (heavy); finished 3.25l behind better fancied stablemate on that occasion but looks like stable number one today.
runners.comment: Shaped like he would improve for the experience when third on debut; stable number one

runners.name: Great Barrier Reef
runners.spotlight: No Nay Never colt; brother to four winners inc Mystery Power (6f-1m1f inc 2yo Group 2; RPR 111), Pimlico (1m; 80) and Unhidden Gem (US 6.5f turf), half-brother to four more inc Fayez (6f-1m2f inc 2yo/AW; 106); dam 1m4f/1m5f winner (73); given a good mention by trainer on media morning last month.
runners.comment: No Nay Never colt was given a good mention by trainer at a media morning last month

runners.name: Duke Of Arrakis
runners.spotlight: 40,000euros yearling; second foal; closely related to 7f-8.6f AW winner Hardstyle (RPR 75); dam unplaced 6f-10.6f (63), sister to useful winners Sash (1m) and Onight (7f), out of 1m 2yo winning half-sister to 1m Listed winner Laurel; worth a market check.
runners.comment: Closely related to 7f-8.6f AW winner Hardstyle (RPR 75); check market

runners.name: Frogs Hollow Creek
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to winners Apprentice (1m1f; RPR 76) and Candella (1m2f AW; 75); dam unplaced 6.5f-1m 2yo in France, half-sister to Racing Post Trophy/St Leger winner Kingston Hill, out of French 10.5f Group 3 winner; unlikely to make an immediate impact.
runners.comment: Half-brother to two winners (RPR 76/75); unlikely to make immediate impact

runners.name: Ischgl
runners.spotlight: Dam 7f 2yo winner (RPR 79), out of unraced half-sister to 7f Group 3 winner Berg Bahn; others appeal more on paper.
runners.comment: Dam 7f 2yo winner (RPR 79); others appeal more on paper

runners.name: Santerno
runners.spotlight: Starspangledbanner colt; dam unplaced 7f/1m (RPR 58), half-sister to French 1m2f 2yo Group 1 winner Tennessee Stud, out of twice-raced half-sister to 7f/1m Group 1 winner Rock Of Gibraltar; check market for debut.
runners.comment: Out of a half-sister to Gr.1 winner Tennessee Stud; check market for debut

runners.name: New Mexico
runners.spotlight: One of two for trainer; 105,000gns yearling was beaten 16l on debut over 5f here earlier this month; may leave that run behind but big improvement needed.
runners.comment: Needs to leave a poor debut run behind him after finishing 16l behind over 5f here
runners.owner: Giselle De Aguiar & AMO Racing Limited

races.race_name: Final Furlong Podcast With 1xBet.ie Handicap

runners.name: Art Of Unity
runners.spotlight: Veteran is 5-61 on turf; was subsequently disqualified for the presence of a banned substance after coming home in front at Bellewstown in October (5f, soft); good effort on stable debut back from a break back at Bellewstown (5f, soft) last time when 1.25l behind Alnofoor.
runners.comment: 5-61 on turf and made a good stable debut last time when 1.25l behind winner Alnofoor

runners.name: Alnofoor
runners.spotlight: Returned from over four years off with some moderate runs last year; however has returned to turf this term in the form of his life, winning back-to-back at Navan (6f, heavy) under Colin Keane and Bellewstown (5f, soft) under a 3lb claimer; rating has risen 14lb but he's still lightly raced and soft conditions will suit.
runners.comment: Back-to-back winner in last two runs; up aggregate of 14lb but still lightly raced

runners.name: Aboveheronlysky
runners.spotlight: Outran long odds on debut at Navan in 2024 when third of 19 but missed all last season; moderate comeback run over C&D (6f, heavy); slight improvement here next time but still ninth of 12 (soft to heavy); tongue tie goes on now for handicap debut.
runners.comment: Outran long odds on debut in 2024 but two runs this year have been moderate

runners.name: Go Out
runners.spotlight: Some good AW runs, runner-up in three races back-to-back in two handicaps off 73 over 6f/7f and in a 7f maiden; not his best effort in a 6f handicap in March and turf debut over C&D (heavy) later that month wasn't anything to write home about either.
runners.comment: Good efforts on AW but not his best effort on turf debut over C&D last time
runners.owner: Mrs John Murphy & John Pearson & Karl Kirwan

runners.name: Blue Anthem
runners.spotlight: Three wins for George Boughey (two on AW), signing off with a 6f AW second at Wolverhampton; bought for 21,000gns in October and too keen on yard debut at Dundalk in March (7f); never dangerous back there later that month; improvement needed.
runners.comment: Three-time winner for George Boughey but moderate in two AW runs for new yard

runners.name: Rappell
runners.spotlight: Three turf wins, the latest coming over C&D in 2024 off 66 (good); added a 6f AW win since then last September; moderate effort back from a break at Dundalk last time; all wins have come on better ground.
runners.comment: Three turf wins, the latest over C&D in 2024 but all wins came on better ground

runners.name: Eichan San
runners.spotlight: Decent efforts in turf maidens last year at 6f-1m; seventh and 1.5l behind Reposado over 6f at Naas in March (6f, soft) when it looked an inadequate trip for him; reported to be blowing hard post race here over 7f last time when disappointing (7f, soft to heavy); wouldn't be surprising if he posted an improved performance.
runners.comment: Some decent form but blowing hard when disappointing last time; no surprise if he improves

runners.name: Reposado
runners.spotlight: Winless from 13 runs; some decent juvenile form; good runner-up finish at Dundalk on handicap debut in March (6f) and creditable run back on turf at Naas later that month (6f, soft); perhaps better suited to better ground.
runners.comment: Winless from 13; okay recent runs but perhaps better suited to better ground
runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar

runners.name: Andosky
runners.spotlight: Best effort came on Sligo handicap debut in August (6f, good to yielding) when dead-heating for the runner-up spot; no impact at Navan next time (6f, yielding); shaped like she would improve for the run when fifth at Gowran (7f, soft) earlier this month.
runners.comment: Best effort came on h'cap debut; shaped like she would improve on comeback run

runners.name: Carrigans Grove
runners.spotlight: Had a year to remember in 2025, winning three times and placing on a further five occasions from nine runs; neck runner up over C&D in August before going one better under this rider later that month; disappointed as favourite on AW when last seen; brings strong, generally consistent form but softer conditions pose a question.
runners.comment: A year to remember in 2025 winning three times inc' over C&D; softer conditions a question

runners.name: Royal Impact
runners.spotlight: Ordinary form in turf maidens but improved for switch to AW and won on handicap debut over 7f in February off 63; doing his best work at the finish when a 7l fourth here last time (7f, soft to heavy); not certain a drop to 6f is what he wants.
runners.comment: Doing best work at the finish when fourth pver 7f here latest; drop in trip may not suit

runners.name: Astute Power
runners.spotlight: Ordinary form other than a close third of nine in a 5f maiden at Navan in October (soft) with an RPR of 77; hasn't matched that level in two runs since, but claims if he does on handicap bow.
runners.comment: Ordinary m'den form other than close third at Navan; claims if matching that on h'cap bow
runners.owner: Exors of the Late Mrs S Power

runners.name: Bint Majestic Roi
runners.spotlight: Surprisingly off the mark at the ninth attempt when landing a 1m Dundalk handicap in December at 50-1; creditable fifth next time on AW and returned to turf in a C&D fillies' handicap earlier this month with a good third despite being 3lb wrong; player.
runners.comment: AW winner ran well over C&D earlier this month when fourth; player

runners.name: Angel Of Promise
runners.spotlight: Looked raw at Navan on sole start as a 2yo; shaped well when a staying-on sixth of 18 over an extended 6f here on seasonal debut; hasn't builed on that in two subsequent runs here, the latest over 7f in a tongue tie (now abandoned); makes handicap debut now.
runners.comment: Shaped well on seasonal return here, but didn't build on that since; h'cap debutant

runners.name: The Love Machine
runners.spotlight: Navan nursery handicap winner in October (6f, yielding); has beaten precious few rivals in three handicap runs for new yard including when tailed off at Bellewstown last time; new hood needs to spark something.
runners.comment: Navan nursery handicap winner in Oct'; has beaten precious few rivals for new yard

runners.name: Fontaine Khaled
runners.spotlight: Some promise in her three maiden runs last term; always behind on handicap debut at Naas last time (6f, soft); perhaps the added tongue tie can get more from her.
runners.comment: Some promise in her m'dens but always behind on h'cap debut latest
runners.owner: Dan Kiely & C O'Neill & Apollo Thoroughbreds

runners.name: Rosato
runners.spotlight: Three 6f wins with the latest coming at Naas last July off 60 (Carrigans Grove in second) (yielding); has run well over this C&D in October, finished a 6l fifth here (soft to heavy) before a 5.25l third later in the month (6f, heavy); 7f was perhaps too far when last seen here in November; may improve for the run.
runners.comment: Ran well over C&D twice last October; chance but may improve for the run

runners.name: Apothic Red
runners.spotlight: Not devoid of promise on when seventh on debut at Gowran despite a price of 150-1 (7f, good to yielding); subsequent efforts below that including on comeback run at the Curragh last month when a distant eighth of 16; makes handicap debut but others preferred.
runners.comment: Some promise in opening run but subsequent efforts below that; handicap debutant
runners.owner: Mrs Pauline McCourt/Donal O'Leary/Don't Ask The Wife Synd

runners.name: Nouvel Espoir
runners.spotlight: Gained all three wins over the minimum trip, including here in June last year (good to yielding) off 63; ran well at Navan twice towards the end of last year but interspersed with two moderate starts at this venue; this will be just the third ride of his rider's career under rules'.
runners.comment: Gained all three wins over 5f; inexperienced claimer aboard
runners.owner: Ms Yvonne Hennessy & Ms S O'Neill

runners.name: William F Browne
runners.spotlight: Lost his way last season after going close in a 1m handicap at Galway in August (yielding) off 4lb higher; perhaps the break and the added tongue tie will aid a return to form.
runners.comment: Lost way last season after going close in a Galway festival h'cap; break may have helped

runners.name: Oppenheimer
runners.spotlight: Never counted in opening two maidens here (6.5f, good) and at Leopardstown (7f, good); keen and led before being headed from 1f out at Naas last time when fading into seventh (6f, yielding to soft); may do better now handicapping.
runners.comment: Led before fading into seventh at Naas last time; may do better now handicapping

races.race_name: Tote Guarantee, Never Beaten By SP Alleged Stakes (Group 3)

runners.name: Beset
runners.spotlight: Ended last season on a high with 1m4f Listed win at Naas in a race in which stablemate Beset floped; maintained progression at Naas on return; more needed now.
runners.comment: Ended last season on a high with 1m4f Listed win, maintained progression at Naas on return
runners.owner: K Blake/Mrs E Palmer/M Wilson/N Luck

runners.name: Twain
runners.spotlight: Unbeaten in two races at two; going was heavy for Group 1 Saint-Cloud win from stablemate Mount Kilimanjaro; missed all of last season; a fascinating runner on the comeback trail now.
runners.comment: Unbeaten in two races at two, going was heavy for Group 1 Saint-Cloud win, unraced in 2025
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Trustyourinstinct
runners.spotlight: Good form early last season, notably recording back-to-back wins at this venue in a Listed 1m4f contest and Group 3 International Stakes over this trip; below best when attempting a repeat of his 2024 Group 3 win at Leopardstown's Irish Champions meeting; well beaten at the same venue on final start.
runners.comment: Good early-season form at the track in 2025, well below best in two starts in the autumn

runners.name: Royal Rhyme
runners.spotlight: Won the Brigadier Gerard Stakes on his return in 2024; later, generally found things too demanding at the highest level (sixth in the International, seventh of eight in the Irish Champion) before finishing with a career-best in taking third behind Anmaat in the Champion Stakes; ran only twice last season (both Group 1s); pick of this field on best form; effective on soft.
runners.comment: Ran only twice last year, the pick of the field on his Group 1 form, effective on soft
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Green Impact
runners.spotlight: Beat the top-class Delacroix in Group 2 at Leopardstown at two (1m, good); sixth in 2,000 Guineas before making all in Listed race at Leopardstown last June (1m1f, good); respectable sixth in the Irish Derby (1m4f, good); front-running effort petered out when fourth in York Group 2 event, though was best of the Classic generation; should make a smart middle distance 4yo.
runners.comment: Group 2 winner at two, Listed scorer at three, should make a smart middle distance 4yo

runners.name: Starford
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner, progressive in handicaps last season; unplaced in a race won by stablemate Beset on his only stakes race attempt; plenty to prove as a result.
runners.comment: Three-time winner, unplaced in a race won by Beset on his only stakes race attempt

runners.name: Carolina Jetstream
runners.spotlight: Boasts useful form at Dundalk, the scene of all four wins; limited turf experience; major improvement needed to make an impact at this level.
runners.comment: Boasts useful form at Dundalk, major improvement needed to make an impact at this level
runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar

runners.name: Red Letter
runners.spotlight: Fourth in Group 1 Moyglare Stakes; came good after being beaten by the smart Barnavara at Navan last June; handled soft when landing 1m Listed race at Killarney; left an impression she would stay further with 1m1f course win last August; turned the tables on Barnavara then, but that rival prevailed again here in September; should be in the mix.
runners.comment: Showed a consistent standard of form at a high level last season, should be in the mix

runners.name: Eastwatch
runners.spotlight: Two wins over extended 1m1f at Gowran last year (maiden/conditions race); failed to make an impact at Listed level next two starts over further; runner-up in Irish Lincoln on return; unplaced favourite for a 1m2f handicap last time; up against it at this level.
runners.comment: Runner-up in Irish Lincoln on penultimate start, weak form in Listed races, up against it

runners.name: Bubble Gum
runners.spotlight: Finished midfield in French and German Oaks in 2024; Listed winner at Cork for Paddy Twomey 12 months ago; below that form when fifth in Munster Oaks last June; never showed with a chance over 1m here on stable/seasonal debut; unlikely to feature
runners.comment: Never showed with a chance over 1m here on stable/seasonal debut, unlikely to feature

runners.name: Omni Man
runners.spotlight: Ended last year with a Listed AW third; one of three runners for the stable; will need to raise his game to feature here
runners.comment: Ended last year with a Listed AW third, will need to raise his game to feature here

runners.name: Edward Hamilton
runners.spotlight: Frankel colt made a belated winning debut in an ordinary Dundalk AW maiden last September when allowed to dictate; never showed with a chance in a Listed race at Leopardstown; tried with blinkers; stable's second string, best watched.
runners.comment: Dundalk maiden winner, no threat in a Listed race on only other star, yard's second string

races.race_name: Bermingham Cameras Irish EBF Maiden

runners.name: Cactus
runners.spotlight: Well exposed daughter of Wootton Bassett out of a mare by Galileo; regressive after placed efforts over 6f at Navan last August/September; hard to be confident despite the pedigree and connections,
runners.comment: Regressive after 6f placed efforts at Navan last August/September, hard to be confident

runners.name: Thenandnow
runners.spotlight: Officially rated 75 after four runs at two, the best of which was a 1 3/4 second of eight on debut at Leopardstown (7f, good-to-fim); well beaten both starts on easier ground at 6f-7f; the going is a potential negative but slightly superior to Cactus and Glen And Tonic on official figures
runners.comment: The going is a potential negative but official rating of 75 merits respect in a weak race

runners.name: Starman Tom
runners.spotlight: One of the more obvious candidates based on his recent debut second over 5f at Cork; the front-running winner brought a high standard to that race, having finished fourth behind True Love in last season's Queen Mary.
runners.comment: One of the more obvious candidates based on his recent debut second over 5f at Cork
runners.owner: Poor Fools Syndicate/Deirdre Cotter/Thomas W. McGrath

runners.name: Glen And Tonic
runners.spotlight: Improved on modest turf maiden form when third over 5f at Dundalk on AW/nursery debut last August; backed that up over 6f at the same venue on her next start; unplaced favourite in a Dundalk maiden; official rating of 71 should not be good enough to win a maiden at this venue, but this race looks weak
runners.comment: Fair AW form, 71 official rating ought to be a negative but this is a weak Curragh maiden

runners.name: Magna Kotcha
runners.spotlight: Not much impression on debut at Naas; no improvement for Colin Keane at Cork.
runners.comment: Not much impression on debut at Naas, no improvement for Colin Keane at Cork

runners.name: Gilts
runners.spotlight: E8,000 yearling; first foal; dam a winner in Italy, from the family of Goldikova; one of two newcomers for the stable; best watched.
runners.comment: Dam won in Italy, one of two newcomers for the stable, best watched

runners.name: Grande Cuvee
runners.spotlight: 11th foal; sister to three winners including Grande Marques, a 5f Listed scorer for this stable; half-sister to six winners; trainer and rider involved with a Group 3 winner at Leopardstown last Sunday.
runners.comment: 11th foa; dam has produced nine winners including a Listed-winning ssiter to this filly

runners.name: Miss Moore Cen
runners.spotlight: E30,000 3yo; tenth foal; sister to three winners and half-sister to four winners; dam Flat/hurdle winner; very capable young trainer, though yet to get off the mark for the turf season.
runners.comment: Sister to three winners and half-sister to four winners, very capable young trainer

runners.name: A Good Yarn
runners.spotlight: First foal; Calyx gelding whose dam (by Australia) was 7f AW 2yo/1m turf winner, sister to 6f/7f winner Back Down Under, closely related to 1m1f Flat/2m hurdle winner Champion Green; Wayne Lordan is a noteworthy booking for McManus homebred Calyx gelding.
runners.comment: Wayne Lordan is a noteworthy booking for homebred Calyx gelding, winning dam by Australia

runners.name: Cool Azul
runners.spotlight: E30,000 yearling; fifth foal; half-brother to winners, notably Last Crusader, a 5f Listed scorer for Karl Burke; dam French Group-placed 6f 2yo winner.
runners.comment: Half-brother to Last Crusader a 5f Listed scorer for Karl Burke, dam Group-placed winner

runners.name: Ragaire
runners.spotlight: 85,000gns foal, 400,000gns yearling; third foal; brother to 6f winner Brigid's Cloak; dam 1m/1m2f winner by Arakan, half-sister to 1m1f Group 3 winner Enforcer; increase in purchase price from foal to yearling is noteworthy, makes belated debut
runners.comment: 85,000gns foal, 400,000gns yearling, brother to 6f winner Brigid's Cloak,  belated debut

runners.name: Tide Of Fortune
runners.spotlight: Eighth foal; half-brother to winners, best of whom Silverkode achieved a 104 RPR; one of two newcomers for the stable.
runners.comment: Eighth foal, half-brother to winners, best of whom Silverkode achieved a 104 RPR

runners.name: Classical Symphony
runners.spotlight: In rear on only run at two; unraced last year; struggled over 5f at Cork on reappearance.
runners.comment: In rear on only run at two, unraced last year, struggled over 5f at Cork on reappearance

runners.name: Sweet Baby Zou
runners.spotlight: No sign of winning potential after two runs; more likely a handicap prospect in due course.
runners.comment: No sign of winning potential after two runs, more likely a handicap prospect in due course

runners.name: Ochtar Againn
runners.spotlight: Never figured over C&D (heavy) on sole start last year; much the same here on reappearance.
runners.comment: Never figured over C&D on sole start last year, much the same here on reappearance.

runners.name: Cantilever
runners.spotlight: E125,000 yearling; No Nay Never colt; mid-field at Naas on only start at two. needs to improve from last month's C&D fifth.
runners.comment: Mid-field at Naas on only start at two, needs to improve from last month's C&D fifth
runners.owner: M R Sabatine & Mrs P Shanahan & C McLiney & S T Tsang

runners.name: Bartolito
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in races at Bellewstown and Dundalk; not a big price on the latter occasion, but can be left out of calculations here.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten in races at Bellewstown and Dundalk, can be left out of calculations

runners.name: Concaire
runners.spotlight: Will need to pick up more experience on the evidence of his recent introduction at Dundalk.
runners.comment: Will need to pick up more experience on the evidence of his introduction at Dundalk.

runners.name: New Divide
runners.spotlight: Failed to improve from an initial C&D run when stepped up to 7f at Naas.
runners.comment: Failed to improve from an initial C&D run when stepped up to 7f at Naas

runners.name: Dreaminthejungle
runners.spotlight: Shaped promisingly when seventh of 16 over 6f at this venue on only start last year; faces a big task against the 3yos.
runners.comment: Shaped promisingly at this venue on only start last year, big task against the 3yos here

runners.name: Lar Na Coille
runners.spotlight: Two widely-spaced runs at two; fair sixth of 16 at Navan offers some limited encouragement.
runners.comment: Two widely-spaced runs at two, fair sixth of 16 at Navan offers some encouragement
runners.owner: Daragh McCormack & Mrs J S Bolger

runners.name: Threebiggulps
runners.spotlight: Slow to break and never in contention first time out at Dundalk; makes no appeal.
runners.comment: Slow to break and never in contention first time out at Dundalk, makes no appeal

runners.name: Spiritofthedawn
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced at Leopardstown on only start at two; hood and tongue-tie on now.
runners.comment: Not disgraced at Leopardstown on only start at two, hood and tongue-tie on now

runners.name: Changing Direction
runners.spotlight: Struggled in sprint-trip maidens at Naas and Navan last season; safe to rule out.
runners.comment: Struggled in sprint-trip maidens at Naas and Navan last season, safe to rule out

races.race_name: 1xBet.ie Supports Safe Gambling Handicap

runners.name: Local Lad
runners.spotlight: Couple of good runs in quality 2yo maidens but didn't match that level in five runs last season; may improve now switched to handicaps.
runners.comment: Some good 2yo m'den runs but didn't match that last season; switched to h'caps now

runners.name: Goal Exceeded
runners.spotlight: Dual turf winner with the latest of those coming on stable debut at the Curragh in May last year off 73 (7f, good); struggled off revised mark for remainder of the season but back to form over 7f at Leopardstown on seasonal return four days ago when second; needs to back that up.
runners.comment: Dual winner inc' C&D; needs to back up good runner-up finish at Leopardstown last time
runners.owner: Ms H McGuinness/Martin W White/Declan Sheridan/Breen White

runners.name: San Aer
runners.spotlight: Four wins on the Flat for Tom Mullins, including over C&D in 2022; finished runner-up behind subsequent Irish Lincoln winner Ribee at Galway in October (1m1f, soft to heavy); poor on return to action at Naas last month; could improve.
runners.comment: Runner-up behind subsequent Irish Lincoln winner in Oct'; can improve over poor comeback

runners.name: Smoke Them Out
runners.spotlight: Notched a third career win when making all on seasonal return over 6f here in March (heavy); 5f may not have suited last time when 7.75l behind (soft to heavy); upped in trip now and could be competitive.
runners.comment: Third career win here over 6f last month; 5f didn't suit last time; could be competitive

runners.name: L L Koulsty
runners.spotlight: Stepped forward to land a 6f Cork conditions event on his second start in September (yielding to soft); form of that had multiple boosts in behind; took time to settle at Newcastle when failing to justify favouritism last time (1m); cheekpieces go on and drop back in trip should suit.
runners.comment: Form of Cork win was franked; disappointed over 1m at Newcastle last time

runners.name: Mickey The Steel
runners.spotlight: Useful sprinter who is suited by soft conditions; third in opening two 6f runs this season here (heavy) and at Naas (soft); upped in trip when runner-up over C&D last time; was 4.75l behind winner Red Charlie that day but now 14lb swing at the weights, although he had race-fitness on his side that day.
runners.comment: Suited by soft and placed in all three runs this season; 4.75l behind Red Charlie latest

runners.name: Lahore Da King
runners.spotlight: Back-to-back AW winner in December over this trip; hadn't impressed in five previous turf runs but turned that on its head when landing a Bellewstown handicap off 67 eight days ago (1m, soft); can't be ruled out but others preferred.
runners.comment: Back-to-back AW winner added a turf win last time; up 8lb and others preferred

runners.name: Hexagonal
runners.spotlight: Some decent maiden runs in defeat last term; disappointed at Dundalk on handicap bow and latest run at Gowran was not an improvement; cheekpieces worn that day now left off but more needed.
runners.comment: Some decent m'den form but two h'cap runs disappointing; more needed

runners.name: Real Force
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner won on seasonal return here in 2024 over 5f (heavy); couldn't repeat the trick last month when a 5.25l fourth of nine (6f, heavy); only raced twice last season, so encouraging to see him out again and comeback run is something to build on but must shoulder top weight.
runners.comment: Five-time winner was a 5.25l fourth on seasonal return; must carry top weight

runners.name: Jon Riggens
runners.spotlight: Three-time course winner over 5f/6f on varying ground; poor return to action over 6f here last month (heavy); tries 7f now and may improve over comeback run but definitely needs to.
runners.comment: Three-time course winner; poor here last time; tries 7f now but improvement needed

runners.name: Earls
runners.spotlight: Veteran has won 11 races; sole success last year came off 76 at Roscommon (7.5f, soft); beaten 20l on seasonal return over 1m last time (soft to heavy); others preferred.
runners.comment: 11-time winning veteran was beaten 20l here on seasonal return; others preferred

runners.name: Lady Mary Heath
runners.spotlight: Ordinary form last year; promising start to the season when fourth of 26 over 1m here in March (heavy); didn't build on that at Navan when didn't get a clear run (1m2f, heavy); could go well back here.
runners.comment: Promising start to season when fourth over 1m here; didn't build on that last time

runners.name: Merisi Diamond
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner whose best form has on soft and heavy at around 7f; got those optimum conditions over C&D last time when third, finishing 1.75l behind Mickey The Steel and 6.5l behind Red Charlie; cheekpieces added to the tongue tie now, but may struggle to reverse form.
runners.comment: Four-time winner was behind both Mickey The Steel and Red Charlie over C&D last time
runners.owner: David Brennan & J Tuite & Ms Joanna Morgan & Jeremy Greene

runners.name: Red Charlie
runners.spotlight: Winless from five starts for Emmet Mullins in 2024 but returned from a 493-day absence over C&D last month when pulverising the 23-runner field (soft to heavy); handicapper hasn't missed him, handing a whopping 15lb rise; but big chance of going in again with softer ground to suit.
runners.comment: Took a whopping 15lb hit for easy win back from 493-day absence over C&D; big chance

runners.name: Sun Soldier
runners.spotlight: Gained breakthrough success in first-time blinkers at Punchestown (1m, good to yielding) in September off 70; good third off this mark at Cork when last seen in September; blinkers are getting the best from him and he's a player if ready on seasonal return.
runners.comment: Won and placed since application of blinkers; player if ready on return
runners.owner: A Oliver & Blue Candi Lab London Limited

runners.name: Blues Emperor
runners.spotlight: Five wins for Johnny Murtagh but huge odds in three handicap runs for new stable and didn't run well enough to suggest he's well handicapped.
runners.comment: Huge odds in three h'cap runs for new yard and ran moderately

runners.name: Loingseoir
runners.spotlight: Three of seven wins have come at this venue, including over 7f in October off 66 (yielding); little impact on final start here last year (7f, heavy); past form suggests he may improve for this seasonal return.
runners.comment: Three of seven wins came here inc' over 7f in Oct'; may improve for this return
runners.owner: W Keeling & M C Grassick & Mrs M J Grassick

races.race_name: Your Game, Your Odds, Your 1xBet.ie Handicap

runners.name: Gonna Be Golden
runners.spotlight: Game winner on the second of last season's two 7f runs at Dundalk; should be fine with an extra furlong; question mark about soft ground.
runners.comment: Game winner on the second of two 7f runs at Dundalk, should be fine with an extra furlong

runners.name: Dreoilin
runners.spotlight: Weak form in two 1m runs at two; took a step in the right direction when staying on for fourth in a 6f maiden here on seasonal debut; raced a little too keenly over 1m2f last time and beaten 7l in fourth; this trip may suit better.
runners.comment: Raced a little too keenly over 1m2f last time and beaten 7l, this trip may suit better

runners.name: Johnny Soda
runners.spotlight: Barrier trial runner-up; promise in two turf starts before career best at Dundalk in September when 3/4l second; needs to improve considerably from handicap/seasonal debut at Naas.
runners.comment: Fair form at two, needs to improve considerably from handicap/seasonal debut at Naas

runners.name: Boston Benjamin
runners.spotlight: Best of three maiden runs came at Naas over 7f last September when a staying-on fourth; way below that level on first AW try; addition of a tongue-tie seemed to do the trick when he won narrowly at Dundalk on handicap debut; may struggle to cope with an 8lb rise; jockey booking a positive.
runners.comment: Narrow winner at Dundalk on handicap debut, may struggle to cope with an 8lb rise

runners.name: Down The Glen
runners.spotlight: Ended last season with a progressive profile in nurseries; now 8lb higher than when scoring at Galway; effective on soft/heavy; further improvement is feasible.
runners.comment: Ended last season with a progressive profile in nurseries, effective on soft/heavy

runners.name: Divine Believer
runners.spotlight: Two moderate runs at Dundalk, not much better at Naas; may find her level in handicaps; needs to show some spark before becoming a betting proposition.
runners.comment: Two moderate runs at Dundalk, not much better at Naas, may find her level in handicaps

runners.name: Hambelton
runners.spotlight: Ran to a similar level in three maidens last year at 7f-1m on easy ground and on AW; beaten around 8l at Cork on handicap debut; needs to find more; stable in good form lately
runners.comment: Beaten around 8l at Cork on handicap debut, needs to find more, stable in good form lately

runners.name: Mo Anam Chara
runners.spotlight: Two Dundalk runs were much better than previous turf efforts; should be more mature now but others have stronger claims.
runners.comment: Two Dundalk runs were much better than previous turf efforts, should be more mature now

runners.name: Hamiyan
runners.spotlight: Fair form in maidens before 5 1/2l fourth in a Leopardstown nursery; all four juvenile runs were in races won by Ballydoyle inmates; interesting now on the strength of Gowran third behind Ballysax winner and Classic hope Christmas Day.
runners.comment: Fourth in a nursery, big chance on his Gowran third behind Classic hope Christmas Day
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Venetian Star
runners.spotlight: First of two nursery starts brought a promising second at Down Royal; faded in a 7f contest at this venue on final start; has changed stable but Ben Coen keeps the ride.
runners.comment: First of two nursery starts was promising, change of stable but Ben Coen keeps the ride

runners.name: Arctic Assassin
runners.spotlight: Modest juvenile form; has a similar profile to stablemate Pass The Buck; gelded now, first time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Has a similar profile to stablemate Pass The Buck, gelded now, first time cheekpieces

runners.name: Pass The Buck
runners.spotlight: Ordinary form at two, similar profile to stablemate Arctic Assassin; has been gelded, cheekpieces for the first time; others have more persuasive credentials.
runners.comment: Ordinary form at two, similar profile to stablemate Arctic Assassin, gelded/cheekpieces

runners.name: Vantage Code
runners.spotlight: Big price for all three maiden starts at two; has been gelded; chance is not obvious; worth a look to see if the market provides any clues.
runners.comment: Big price for all three maiden starts at two, has been gelded, market may  provide a clue

races.race_name: Prestige Security Executive Protection Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Chase

runners.name: Delgany Deadline
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 6yo who got off the mark at Leicester (2m7f) in February before posting a very good second of four in 3m1f handicap here two weeks ago; drops in trip but on the up so still rates a player for yard firmly among the winners.
runners.comment: Very good second here two weeks ago; drops in trip but on the up so still rates a player

runners.name: Alto Alto
runners.spotlight: All his five victories have been gained here but he arrives under a cloud, last of five in 2m1f handicap chase here last month; yard is back among the winners though so not totally dismissed.
runners.comment: All five wins have been gained here but arrives under a cloud; yard back among the winners

runners.name: Begin The Luck
runners.spotlight: Reliable sort who scored at Leicester (2m4f) in March; not discredited when fifth in 2m5f handicap chase at Newton Abbot 15 days ago so ought to be in the shake-up eased 1lb.
runners.comment: Scored at Leicester; solid fifth at Newton Abbot 15 days ago; ought to be in the shake-up

runners.name: Line Of Descent
runners.spotlight: Scored three times over fences (at up to 2m4f) in the first half of 2023; quite lightly raced since but comes here on the back of a good third of nine in 2m5f handicap chase at Hereford 25 days ago; needs considering with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Back on track when third at Hereford 25 days ago; needs considering with cheekpieces added

runners.name: Angel's Dream
runners.spotlight: Gained a maiden success over fences at Lingfield (2m) in November 2024 but lightly raced since and below par, coming in only third of four over 2m1f here two weeks ago; tried in different headgear now.
runners.comment: Arrives below par, third of four here two weeks ago; tried in different headgear now

races.race_name: Mansell McTaggart Mane Event Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Ballyeaston
runners.spotlight: Consistent for previous yard but seems quirky and hung left en route to second-place finishes over 2m3f/2m5f the last twice; that said, could get his act together on debut for Dan Skelton and it's interesting that Harry Skelton prefers him over the higher-rated Loriko.
runners.comment: Quirky for previous yard but could get his act together on debut for Dan Skelton

runners.name: Shutfrontdoor
runners.spotlight: Raced only four times and has produced form figures of 122 in good-ground attempts, the success in bumper; faller on hurdles debut, then earned her latest silver medal at Kempton (2m) where she was no match for the winner but ran respectably; one of the main players.
runners.comment: Has form figures of 122 on good ground and is one of the main players
runners.owner: J Hinds, A Head & G L Moore

runners.name: Moon Monarch
runners.spotlight: Showed some promise in bumpers but hasn't fared so well in two attempts over hurdles, latest 14 months ago; however, booking of Harry Cobden suggests a bigger effort may be on the way.
runners.comment: Booking of Harry Cobden suggests a bigger effort may be on the way; reappearance

runners.name: Giddyupadingdong
runners.spotlight: Very lightly raced; bumper winner in 2023 for previous yard but has achieved little in two hurdles contests since returning from long absence; handicaps should be more suitable shortly.
runners.comment: Has achieved little in two hurdles contests since returning from long absence

runners.name: Versatile
runners.spotlight: Record of only 1-24 on Flat and only sixth in a couple of low-grade AW handicaps for new stable; brings limited potential to hurdles.
runners.comment: Record of only 1-24 on Flat; brings limited potential to hurdles

runners.name: Khalk'eau Spigao
runners.spotlight: Fontwell form comprises a modest fourth (ran below market expectations) and heavy defeat; needs improvement.
runners.comment: Needs improvement on his Fontwell form
runners.owner: D S Dennis & John Hw Finch

runners.name: Oblemenko
runners.spotlight: Comfortably held in two Irish points and a C&D maiden (behind Loriko); best watched unless the market suggests he's likely to take a step forward.
runners.comment: Comfortably held in Irish points and a C&D maiden

races.race_name: BetGoodwin Sussex Stayers' Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Tranquil Sea
runners.spotlight: Had further wind surgery prior to a poor effort last time (2m5f) but may benefit from this step back up in trip and he has become attractively treated, being 3lb below the mark he defied in October (2m7f); Harry Skelton prefers him over the improving Top Guy; interesting.
runners.comment: Has become attractively treated and Harry Skelton prefers him over the improving Top Guy

runners.name: Top Guy
runners.spotlight: Broadly progressive form since November features two wins, including at Newton Abbot (3m2f, good) latest start when doing it readily; may continue to improve; one to consider, despite going up a further 10lb and Harry Skelton preferring stablemate Tranquil Sea.
runners.comment: Broadly progressive form since November; scored readily at Newton Abbot last time

runners.name: Bethpage
runners.spotlight: Fairly useful mare who has Listed form but was never going well en route to a modest third at Ascot (2m5f) when back from a layoff last time; will need to travel better in this competitive affair.
runners.comment: Never going well en route to a modest third last time; needs to travel better

runners.name: Moveit Like Minnie
runners.spotlight: Justified favouritism at Wincanton (2m4f, good) last time, recording a first win over hurdles since 2023; fairly consistent and looks worth a crack at this new trip so may remain competitive; whether he can follow up is a different matter.
runners.comment: Won at Wincanton last time; fairly consistent and may remain competitive
runners.owner: F J Mills & W R Mills

runners.name: Double Powerful
runners.spotlight: Good second in major handicap at Kempton (2m5f) in January, putting a few non-completions (fences/hurdles) behind him; has performed below market expectations in two starts since but is a likely player provided he bounces back with usual hood removed.
runners.comment: Likely player provided he bounces back with usual hood removed; capable of useful form

runners.name: East India Express
runners.spotlight: Disappointing favourite back at the Cheltenham Festival (2m4f); otherwise a useful sort and had returned to form with close second at Windsor the time before; not written off, albeit has stamina to prove back up in distance.
runners.comment: Useful sort who can't be written off, albeit has stamina to prove back up in distance

runners.name: Kamsinas
runners.spotlight: Has faced a couple of difficult tasks since returned to hurdles but was beaten when taking a late fall returned to handicap level most recently; can be opposed off current mark in this deeper field.
runners.comment: Beaten when taking a late fall last time; can be opposed in this deeper field

runners.name: Fountains Blenhein
runners.spotlight: Proving a solid novice hurdler; scored in October/January and ran well under big penalty the last twice, beaten only a neck at Kempton (2m5f, good) latest occasion; bred to be as effective back up in trip; looks worth his mark and has clear possibilities on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Proving a solid novice hurdler; looks worth his mark; possibilities on handicap debut

runners.name: Elysian Knight
runners.spotlight: Record of 0-4 switched to fences this term, albeit far from disgraced on two occasions; 2-6 over hurdles and finished a creditable second at Plumpton (2m4f) last April in most recent attempt; each-way possibilities.
runners.comment: Creditable second at Plumpton last April in most recent hurdles attempt

runners.name: Roger Pol
runners.spotlight: Absent since an October double (2m7f Class 3) that took his hurdles record to 4-12; returns to action off a stiffer mark in deeper waters and looks by no means certain to bag the hat-trick.
runners.comment: Absent since October double; returns to action off a stiffer mark in deeper waters

races.race_name: Weatherbys nhstallions.co.uk Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Morfee
runners.spotlight: Has three wins over fences, including one over C&D; not operating at his best this year and a pull in the weights from when third over C&D in February (soft) should not see him reverse placings with either Lumi Plugin or Enjoy d'Allen.
runners.comment: Has three wins over fences, including one over C&D; not operating at his best this year.

runners.name: Roe And Co
runners.spotlight: Landed a three-timer in November/December but was pulled up on soft when favourite at Fontwell (3m2f) in January when he was reported to have bled from the nose; returns from a break and needs to hit the ground running to get back on track.
runners.comment: Landed a three-timer in November/December but was pulled up at Fontwell in January.

runners.name: Enjoy d'Allen
runners.spotlight: Veteran chaser who landed his third win of the season, over C&D (soft) in February when getting the better of Lumi Plugin; found the Sussex National back here 13 days ago a tad too competitive but cannot be dismissed here back in the right grade.
runners.comment: Veteran chaser who landed his third win of the season, over C&D (soft) in February.

runners.name: Sea Invasion
runners.spotlight: Both wins over fences have been over C&D, the first in first-time headgear a year ago; not at his best when fifth in the Sussex National back here 13 days ago but must be taken seriously down in grade with visor replacing blinkers.
runners.comment: Both wins over fences have been over C&D; drops in grade; taken very seriously

runners.name: Lumi Plugin
runners.spotlight: Has taken well to fences this term and opened his account when scoring over C&D (soft) in January; ran up to that form when runner-up back over C&D and at Kempton since and must hold sound claims.
runners.comment: Has taken well to fences this term and opened his account over C&D in January.
runners.owner: J Hinds, A Head & G L Moore

runners.name: Northern Poet
runners.spotlight: Has five wins over fences, including over C&D, but has been pulled up on his last three starts; back on last winning mark and first-time visor now goes on but he comes with risks.
runners.comment: Has five wins over fences but has been pulled up on his last three starts.

races.race_name: Diane And Harry's Plumpton Annual Box Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Market House
runners.spotlight: On a losing run but he has been running well on the Flat and returned to hurdling with a good second of eight in 2m4f handicap here two weeks ago; must enter calculations nudged up 1lb in a change of headgear.
runners.comment: Good second here two weeks ago; must enter calculations up 1lb in a change of headgear

runners.name: Shalfa
runners.spotlight: Fair 1m2f Flat scorer; progressing really well in this sphere and she cosily got off the mark over 2m2f here two weeks ago; up 3lb but this strong-travelling sort is capable of better still; leading claims.
runners.comment: Progressing well and cosily off the mark here latest; more to offer and leading claims

runners.name: Lord Of The Glance
runners.spotlight: On the up since sent into handicaps and he readily landed 11-runner event at Stratford (2m) 22 days ago, forging clear from final flight; hiked up 9lb but still merits serious consideration.
runners.comment: Ready Stratford scorer 22 days ago; hiked up 9lb but still merits serious consideration

runners.name: Fascinating Lips
runners.spotlight: A fair 2m2f hurdles winner at his best but he ended his time with Gary and Josh Moore out of sorts before Christmas; pulled up on his yard debut over C&D recently and tongue-tie is added here.
runners.comment: Ended time with Gary and Josh Moore out of sorts; pulled up over C&D on recent yard debut

runners.name: Dissident
runners.spotlight: Opened his account in Doncaster juvenile hurdle (2m) in December; well below par subsequently though, back on the Flat when tenth at Doncaster 22 days ago; change of headgear now tried.
runners.comment: Arrives well below par, tenth back on Flat at Doncaster latest; change of headgear tried

runners.name: Alibey
runners.spotlight: Capable of fair form (for William Haggas) on the Flat; yet to match that in this sphere for her current handler, again going too freely when a fading fourth of 11 in 2m Stratford novice 22 days ago; remains with potential on her handicap debut though if becoming more tractable.
runners.comment: Too free when fading Stratford fourth latest; remains with potential on h'cap debut though

runners.name: Banksman
runners.spotlight: A fair 1m Flat winner for Jack Channon last summer but yet to fire for his current yard under both codes, tried in cheekpieces when eighth in 1m4f AW Lingfield handicap in January; not easy to make a case for.
runners.comment: Yet to fire for his current yard under both codes; not easy to make a case for

runners.name: Answer That
runners.spotlight: A fair ex-Irish bumper performer who recorded easily his best effort over hurdles when third of five in novice over C&D 13 days ago; well in the mix with likely better still to come now handicapping.
runners.comment: Easily best effort over hurdles when C&D third latest; well in the mix now handicapping

runners.name: Mystical Mabel
runners.spotlight: Well held on the Flat and she has also offered little promise since sent hurdling, only tenth in Newbury juvenile in November; makes her handicap debut after a layoff.
runners.comment: Offered little promise over hurdles, tenth at Newbury in November; handicap debutante

runners.name: Chartwell Jock
runners.spotlight: A fair 1m2f Flat winner for Andrew Balding; yet to show any form of note in three juvenile hurdles this winter though and needs this switch to handicap company to yield major progress.
runners.comment: Fair Flat winner for Andrew Balding; yet to show any form of note in a trio of hurdles

races.race_name: Kalm Club Keep Calm & Gallop On Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Notnowlinda
runners.spotlight: Skelton mare who showed major signs of a revival in first-time cheekpieces at Uttoxeter (2m4f) most recently, finishing a close third; remains 3lb below the mark she defied at Hereford (2m) two years ago; warrants respect in the retained headgear.
runners.comment: Showed major signs of a revival in first-time cheekpieces most recently; respected

runners.name: The Bellhop
runners.spotlight: Juvenile hurdler whose consistent form features two wins over 2m1f at Fontwell; ran creditably in 4yo handicaps at Sandown and Ascot the last twice; gives the impression he's ready for a crack at this new trip; likely player, taking on elders who are largely exposed.
runners.comment: Juvenile hurdler who has consistent form; looks ready for a crack at this new trip

runners.name: Hecouldbetheone
runners.spotlight: Spell over fences featured two wins last June; unable to take advantage of drop to selling level back over hurdles last time but won this contest off 5lb higher 12 months ago (admittedly beat only two rivals) and has a fighting chance provided he's back in similar form.
runners.comment: Won this contest off 5lb higher 12 months ago; fighting chance if back in similar form

runners.name: Goodwin
runners.spotlight: Needs to rebound, having been out of form since the turn of the year; however, has a record of 211 at Plumpton, most recently winning off just 1lb lower in November; return to this venue may have positive effect; goes without headgear for first time in a while.
runners.comment: Needs to rebound but his record of 211 at Plumpton augurs well; headgear removed

runners.name: Oakley's Way
runners.spotlight: Solid record in 2m3f-2m5f attempts comprises an account-opening success at Taunton (good to firm) in November and second-place finishes, including at Plumpton, in last two starts; may do better still at this sort of distance; solid claims.
runners.comment: Latest effort took his record at 2m3f-2m5f to 122; may do better still; solid claims

runners.name: Alien Storm
runners.spotlight: Came up short last time when bidding for a chase hat-trick; only 1-11 over hurdles and hasn't raced in this sphere for nearly two years but there are positives, namely a lower mark in this sphere and a good overall record (4-8) at Plumpton.
runners.comment: Not seen over hurdles for nearly two years but is on an attractive mark; 4-8 at Plumpton

races.race_name: BetGoodwin Mares' Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 2 Elimination) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Posh Chick
runners.spotlight: Successful in the first of two attempts in points; filled the runner-up spot at Wincanton (good) on the switch to rules; one of the main form contenders in this field, along with stablemate Steal A Glance.
runners.comment: Point winner; runner-up at Wincanton on the switch to rules; major player

runners.name: Steal A Glance
runners.spotlight: Out of a bumper/hurdle winner for her yard; faded into midfield at Wincanton, failing to back up her close second at Ludlow, but this looks weaker and she may yet progress; shortlist material.
runners.comment: Close second at Ludlow then midfield at Wincanton; this looks weaker

runners.name: Silver Whisper
runners.spotlight: £5,000 3yo; unraced dam closely related to useful winners Whisperinthebreeze (2m hurdle/2m5f chase) and Silver Kayf (2m3f/2m5f hurdle), out of bumper scorer; stable's respectable record in bumpers this term includes winning newcomers; Sean Bowen booked; interesting.
runners.comment: Stable's respectable record in bumpers this term includes winning newcomers

runners.name: Mirabello
runners.spotlight: Finished tailed-off last of five at Ffos Las but the soft ground possibly didn't suit and/or something was amiss; in good hands and from a useful family; may leave that effort behind.
runners.comment: From a useful family and may leave her Ffos Las effort behind

runners.name: Cheese
runners.spotlight: Dam 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase winner, half-sister to French 2m2f-2m4f hurdle/chase scorer Great Parade; yard has a poor strike-rate in bumpers; looks one for jump races in due course.
runners.comment: Bred for jumps and yard has a poor strike-rate in bumpers

runners.name: Golden Sanjida
runners.spotlight: Modest fourth at Fakenham then failed to beat a rival at Huntingdon; fitting of hood needs to make a big difference.
runners.comment: Fitting of hood needs to make a big difference

runners.name: Skye Glance
runners.spotlight: Huntingdon form comprises a non-completion (brought down) and eighth of nine; needs improvement on the latter effort.
runners.comment: Needs improvement on her Huntingdon form

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Mares Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Ballyhahill
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam 1m1f-1m4f Flat winner and useful 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase winner, half-sister to winners Opposition (1m4f) and Dutchartcollector (1m 2yo); okay debut in 2m Limerick 4yo fillies' maiden last month, tiring before last; won't have to improve too much to play a major role here.
runners.comment: Okay debut at Limerick last month, won't have to improve too much to play a major role
runners.owner: David Spratt & Kevin McMunigal & Mark Hopkins

runners.name: Crohan Lady
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner; improved for a switch to handicaps in February when taking a close third at Thurles (2m1f, soft to heavy), ordinary enough efforts twice since in handicaps; cheekpieces now added.
runners.comment: Ordinary handicap form; cheekpieces now added on return to maiden company

runners.name: Evelyn
runners.spotlight: Has posted some decent efforts without winning in bumpers and maiden hurdles but well held in handicap latest; wants better ground.
runners.comment: Not out of it on her best form but wants better ground

runners.name: Nirisque
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to several French jumps winners; dam French 1m7f-2m2f hurdle/chase winner (inc Grade 3 hurdle); runner-up in a 2m2f hurdle race in France before joining Henry de Bromhead, easy enough to back when well held in 2m3f Naas maiden in December; improvement expected.
runners.comment: French runner-up all the better for Irish debut run in December; don't rule out

runners.name: Delorean Dynamite
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in three bumpers, most recently on yard debut last month at Down Royal (keen); switches to hurdles and needs to settle better.
runners.comment: Too keen on last month's yard debut after a break; switches to hurdles now

runners.name: Chautuaqua
runners.spotlight: Clonmel bumper win came on heavy, now 0-13 hurdling, pick of recent form when second on similar ground in Gowran mares maiden last month; didn't seem to stay 2m3f on latest so return to 2m to suit; claims.
runners.comment: Didn't seem to stay 2m3f on latest so return to 2m to suit; claims

runners.name: Cool Summer
runners.spotlight: Mild promise on hurdles debut at Roscommon last summer (good-to-yielding) but well held twice afterwards, heavy ground may not have suited second occasion; lack of a recent run a concern as is ground.
runners.comment: Lack of a recent run a concern and may want better ground

runners.name: Likeyouhaveplenty
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam 2m hurdle winner, out of dual bumper winner; debutante worth a market check.
runners.comment: First foal of a winning hurdler; worth a market check on debut

runners.name: Dromana Mist
runners.spotlight: Poet's Word filly is fourth foal of a 2m hurdle winner, herself a half-sister to several winners; unlikely one to make an immediate impact.
runners.comment: Poet's Word filly is out of a winning hurdler; unlikely one to make an immediate impact

runners.name: Clash Gold
runners.spotlight: Has failed to build on a mildly promising bumper debut, well held both attempts in 2m4f Fairyhouse maiden hurdles; likely more one for handicaps.
runners.comment: Well held both attempts in 2m4f Fairyhouse maidens; likely more one for handicaps

runners.name: Shirleynightingale
runners.spotlight: Sister to decent 2m4f-2m6f hurdle/chase winner Bioluminescence, half-sister to winners The Flame (useful 3m hurdle/3m2f chase) and Dollar Bae (bumper/1m7f-2m3f hurdle); dam 2m3f-3m hurdle/chase winner; never sighted on last month's debut in Limerick fillies' maiden, plenty to find with Ballyhahill on that run.
runners.comment: Never sighted on last month's Limerick debut, plenty to find with Ballyhahill

runners.name: Cinnabar Moth
runners.spotlight: Three bumper runs haven't amounted to a great deal, hood tried here when last seen in May last year now left off for hurdles debut.
runners.comment: Three modest bumper runs; hood tried here when last seen in May left off for hurdles debut

races.race_name: Copper Coast Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Polepatrick
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 6yo showed definite ability in bumpers for Dermot McLoughlin, notably in decent Navan contest (soft-to-heavy); returned from over year's absence for current yard last month when hooded in 2m Clonmel maiden hurdle, staying on third behind an odds-on shot (soft); hood left off here but rates a major contender.
runners.comment: Recent Clonmel comeback third augurs well; definite player

runners.name: Kentucky Beach
runners.spotlight: Front-runner placed on five of 7 attempts hurdling, notably touched off in Gowran novice handicap and again twice in similar Fairyhouse contests, latest early this month; should make a bold bid back in maiden company on a track which suits his style of running.
runners.comment: Front-runner going close in handicaps of late; should make a bold bid back in a maiden

runners.name: Porcupine Bank
runners.spotlight: Order Of St George 4yo cost 5,000 as a yearling; second foal; dam unraced sister to useful 2m2f/2m4f hurdle winner Alletrix, out of unraced half-sister to high-class hurdler/chaser Aces Four; top yard, has to be respected on debut.
runners.comment: Newcomer from top yard, has to be respected on debut

runners.name: Sea Eagle
runners.spotlight: Dual Flat winner (good/heavy) in 2024 is rated 82 in that sphere although ran nowhere near that figure in Dundalk claimer when last seen in October; previously runner-up in 2m2f Downpatrick maiden hurdle so entitled to respect on that run.
runners.comment: Absent since October, Downpatrick second would give him prospects here

runners.name: Salvador Peps
runners.spotlight: Unseated at the first on debut at Leopardstown; struggled from early at Naas, respiratory noise reported; can only be watched.
runners.comment: Nothing in two maiden runs last month, respiratory noise reported second occasion

runners.name: Lamikal d'Airy
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; beat the only other finisher to win a Borris House maiden point-to-point last month on his third attempt; form probably does not amount to much but has joined a leading stable so any market support noted.
runners.comment: Points' winner has joined Gavin Cromwell; market best guide; reserve

runners.name: Euro Agent
runners.spotlight: Mild promise on second bumper outing for Eoin Griffin, none too fluent and ran out before last on hurdles debut at Leopardstown last month; has since joined Gordon Elliott.
runners.comment: Ran out before last on hurdles debut at Leopardstown last month; has joined Gordon Elliott

runners.name: Let's Go La Fichad
runners.spotlight: Too keen and tailed off in both bumpers but improvement when hooded on last month's hurdles debut at Wexford, although again somewhat headstrong and jumping left when third; likely more to offer if learning to settle.
runners.comment: Headstrong sort but recent Wexford hurdles debut was encouraging
runners.owner: Tinnahinch Construction Limited & Marie Therese Wall

runners.name: Tyson Fury
runners.spotlight: Four Flat wins, the latest in a Dundalk AW claimer last month; two previous runs over hurdles were at Leopardstown, encouraging fourth in 2m maiden but well out of depth in Grade 1 novice at the DRF; drops in grade here, has to enter calculations.
runners.comment: Out of depth in Grade 1 novice at DRF but recent claiming winner respected on maiden run

runners.name: Cascando
runners.spotlight: Poet's Word half-brother to winners Midleton Rare (bumper and 2m3f/2m4f hurdle) and Princess Sophia (2m hurdle); dam remote second in point, sister to bumper/useful 2m6f hurdle winner First Approach; won Ballynoe point-to-point last month (soft), market possibly best guide now on rules debut.
runners.comment: Ballynoe point-to-point win last month (soft), market possibly best guide on rules debut

runners.name: Ballet Queen
runners.spotlight: Flat winner in France both on AW and on turf (heavy) back in 2021; bought from Godolphin for 10,000euros in November 2024 and returned to the track after a five-year absence with promising Punchestown sixth in February, despite taking a hold; interesting now dropped in grade.
runners.comment: Promising Punchestown comeback run after long absence; interesting now dropped in grade

runners.name: Granlahan
runners.spotlight: First reserve; beaten a long way in maidens at Punchestown and Cork.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way in maidens at Punchestown and Cork; reserve

runners.name: Brave Josee
runners.spotlight: No show in maidens at Punchestown and Naas; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: No show in maidens at Punchestown and Naas; hard to fancy

runners.name: Chopsticks
runners.spotlight: 60-rated Flat handicapper whose sole win came at Sligo (1m6f); two recent hurdle runs suggest he'll be of more interest in handicaps in due course.
runners.comment: Flat winner whose two recent hurdle runs suggest he'll be of more interest in handicaps

runners.name: Colesberg
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to winners Celestial Park (2m4f hurdle) and Global Fortune (point); dam unraced sister to useful 2m5f-3m2f hurdle/chase winner Warden Hill; soundly beaten at huge odds in both maiden hurdles last month; easily overlooked.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten at huge odds in both maiden hurdles last month; easily overlooked

runners.name: Lanzhou
runners.spotlight: Mild promise in point-to-points but two maiden hurdle runs don't inspire confidence.
runners.comment: Mild promise in points but two maiden hurdle runs don't inspire confidence

runners.name: Stone Rulya
runners.spotlight: Ordinary on the Flat for Ger Lyons and not too much to take from two maiden hurdle runs at Punchestown.
runners.comment: Ordinary on the Flat; not too much to take from two maiden hurdle runs at Punchestown

races.race_name: Adare Manor Opportunity Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Jackmoon
runners.spotlight: Useful bumper form; rated 110 after four spins over hurdles, his best effort coming on latest at Cork (2m7f,  yielding) when a neck second of 13; likely to make a bold bid dropped back in trip for his local yard.
runners.comment: Rated 110 over hurdles, fine effort at Cork latest, bold bid on the cards for local yard

runners.name: Drombane
runners.spotlight: Disappointed in both bumpers but better effort in a first-time tongue strap on hurdles debut at Clonmel (19.5f, soft) on latest; raced keenly and didn't  jump brilliantly but should improve for that initial experience and rider's 4lb claim a plus.
runners.comment: Poor bumper form, better on hurdles debut in tongue tie, rider's claim a plus

runners.name: Dont Tell Jack
runners.spotlight: 0-4 in chases and rated 111; good third off that mark in a handicap chase at Navan on latest; 0-5 over hurdles but ran well when third behind Ethical Diamond and Kainsbourg in a Punchestown maiden in February 2025; big chance if he repeats that.
runners.comment: Good run off 111 in h'cap chase latest, big chance on Punchestown maiden hurdle third

runners.name: Charlie's Dilemma
runners.spotlight: Rated 106; has placed in six of his nine starts over hurdles; soundly beaten on handicap debut after a layoff at Musselburgh latest; will strip fitter now but more needed back in a maiden in first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Placed in six of nine starts over hurdles, sharper for latest but needs more; cheekpieces

runners.name: Lunar Majesty
runners.spotlight: Mare is rated 98 after four runs over hurdles; beaten 13.5l when third at Clonmel latest (19.5f, soft); jumping has lacked fluency at times and needs to improve plenty to take this.
runners.comment: 0-4 and rated 98, beaten 13.5l when third latest, jumping has lacked fluency at times

runners.name: Teddy's Acre
runners.spotlight: 4yo Affinisea gelding was pulled up on his sole point start at Dromahane; brother to a hurdles winner but is likely best watched on Rules' debut.
runners.comment: 4yo Affinisea gelding, pulled up in sole point, best watched on Rules' debut

runners.name: Mr Master Mac
runners.spotlight: Built on promise of his bumper debut at Roscommon (15.5f, gd-yld) when staying on best to score in a bumper at Sligo (18.5f, gd-yld); off since that August win and softer ground an unknown; should make a hurdler on breeding.
runners.comment: Off since Sligo bumper win last Aug', soft an unknown, should make a hurdler on breeding

runners.name: Carniny
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in both points and no impact when massive odds for both maiden hurdles; seems safe to rule out.
runners.comment: No impact when huge odds for both maiden hurdles, seems safe to rule out

runners.name: Codger's Rock
runners.spotlight: Huge odds for both starts but showed a glimmer of promise on debut at Down Royal and still in touch when falling three out at Fairyhouse; needs to jump better.
runners.comment: Glimmers of promise in both starts at huge odds, needs to jump better

runners.name: Lupin Du Groschene
runners.spotlight: Beaten similar distances in 2m maiden hurdles at Tipperary and Gowran on contrasting ground; steps up in trip; will qualify for handicaps after this.
runners.comment: Beaten around 30l in two maiden hurdles, up in trip, handicaps beckon after this

races.race_name: Anne Valley Walk Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Poppycon
runners.spotlight: Showed ability in point-to-points and took a step in the right direction over hurdles on handicap debut at Wexford last month, chasing winner from two out in 2m contest (soft); upped in trip now.
runners.comment: Wexford second was encouraging, could have more to offer now upped in trip

runners.name: Mollys Dolly
runners.spotlight: First reserve; yet to trouble judge in five attempts hurdling, pulled up on recent return from winter break at Cork.
runners.comment: Yet to trouble judge, pulled up on recent return from winter break at Cork; reserve

runners.name: Coffeys Forge
runners.spotlight: Won Downpatrick beginners' chase for Gordon Elliott last May; off-putting 0-18 record over hurdles, mistake at the last proving costly when runner-up at Clonmel (2m4f, heavy), made respiratory noise when pulled up at Limerick last time; tongue-tie now tried.
runners.comment: Winning chaser but 0-18 hurdling, respiratory noise latest; tongue-tie tried

runners.name: Roccabaker
runners.spotlight: Breakthrough win came here on New Year's Day (2m6f, soft) off 85 and ran well off 9lb higher at Punchestown later same month; both runs since leave her with a fair bit to find and cheekpieces now added to usual tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Needs to improve on recent efforts but won here in January; don't rule out

runners.name: Ellies Rock
runners.spotlight: Finished second to a well-handicapped rival at Punchestown (2m3f) in January and off the mark at Clonmel early this month when dropped to 2m (soft); 6lb rise workable, stamina test questionable.
runners.comment: Recent Clonmel winner at 2m; 6lb rise workable, stamina test questionable

runners.name: King Of Westeros
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point second hasn't played a major role in maiden hurdles, although recent Wexford effort was okay; hasn't looked the strongest of finishers so this sharp track may help on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Hasn't looked the strongest of finishers so this sharp track may help on handicap debut

runners.name: The Cobblers Wife
runners.spotlight: Opening two efforts in handicaps leave her with plenty to find; upped in trip.
runners.comment: Opening two efforts in handicaps leave her with plenty to find; upped in trip

runners.name: Imarajan
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; dual-purpose maiden for Eddie and Patrick Harty; remote fourth on recent yard debut in 2m4f Wexford maiden, expected to come on for that here on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Expected to come on for recent comeback run here on handicap debut; reserve

runners.name: Maciver
runners.spotlight: Reeled off a four-timer (three chases and one handicap hurdle) early last year; began 2026 with an encouraging second over C&D but soon beaten at Cork last month (3m); more needed but goes well here.
runners.comment: Penultimate C&D second gives him claims

runners.name: Larkins Lane
runners.spotlight: Well held so far in handicaps, although not discredited on latest of two runs for new yard at Clonmel when staying on late after an awkward start; may yet have more to offer off this lowly mark.
runners.comment: Not discredited at Clonmel latest; may yet have more to offer off this lowly mark

runners.name: Mullanour
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in this race last year off 91 but remains a maiden; pulled up at Limerick last month; previous Clonmel second much more encouraging so don't rule out off this lowly mark.
runners.comment: Last year's second races off 6lb lower here so respected despite poor recent Clonmel run

runners.name: Hob's Angel
runners.spotlight: Last year's third (off 76) remains a maiden and tailed off over C&D in November when last seen.
runners.comment: Last year's third remains a maiden and tailed off over C&D in November when last seen

runners.name: Appian Way
runners.spotlight: Dual winning hurdler but no wins since late 2022; two efforts since February return have been poor.
runners.comment: No wins since late 2022; two efforts since February return have been poor

runners.name: Topothelane
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner is now 0-7 hurdling, pulled up after making early running on recent return from near two-year absence at Cork; best watched for now.
runners.comment: Pulled up after making early running on return from near two-year absence; best watched

runners.name: Tedworth
runners.spotlight: 12-race maiden hurdler absent since October; usual cheekpieces discarded here, handicapper relenting.
runners.comment: Absent since October; usual cheekpieces discarded here, handicapper relenting

runners.name: Elusive Prince
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner was unable to make the breakthrough under rules for both Henry de Bromhead and Jimmy Barcoe; pulled up recently on yard debut at Wexford.
runners.comment: Longstanding maiden pulled up recently on yard debut at Wexford

runners.name: Quietrunsthedon
runners.spotlight: Didn't count on handicap debut at Punchestown in February when a 50-1 shot; hard to fancy unless market speaks.
runners.comment: Didn't count on handicap debut at Punchestown; hard to fancy unless market speaks

runners.name: Toofareastiswest
runners.spotlight: Useful form at one stage but hasn't won in well over two years and pulled up in four of last five outings; new headgear combination tried.
runners.comment: Hasn't won in over 2 years and pulled up in four of last five; new headgear combination

races.race_name: Core Bullion Traders Handicap Chase

runners.name: Mount Frisco
runners.spotlight: Course winner at 15.5f; seems best on heavy ground and scored twice in those conditions earlier this year, over hurdles at Clonmel (16.5f) and over fences at Gowran (19.5f); capable off this mark and rain would boost his claims.
runners.comment: Course winner, seems best on heavy ground, capable and rain would boost claims

runners.name: Binge Worthy
runners.spotlight: Point and two-time hurdles winner is 0-7 in chases; ran well when a close third at Navan in December and 1lb lower now but tailed off at Fairyhouse last time and needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: 0-7 over fences, ran well at Navan in Dec', below best since, needs to bounce back

runners.name: Aclass
runners.spotlight: Won a C&D (soft) beginners' chase in November in fine style; 8l runner up on next start in a Thurles handicap chase off 1lb higher than he is here; may have needed latest after a 101 day break; could go well for local yard.
runners.comment: C&D winner, may have needed latest after 101 days off, could go well for local yard

runners.name: Doyen Magic
