runners.spotlight: Third reserve; won over hurdles at Clonmel in January; solid runs in defeat the last twice in handicap chases at Clonmel (2m, heavy) and Limerick (2m2f, heavy); place shout but needs to find a bit more to win off this mark.
runners.comment: Solid runs in defeat in chases the last twice, needs more to win off this mark; reserve

runners.name: Casey West
runners.spotlight: Sole success came in a hunter chase at Clonmel (2m5f, heavy); not beaten far in two of seven runs this season, including on latest at Limerick (2m7f, heavy), but consistency isn't a strong suit; cheekpieces return.
runners.comment: Not beaten far in two of 7 starts this season inc' on latest, not certain to back that up

runners.name: Sammy Smart
runners.spotlight: Shed the maiden tag over fences at Punchestown (19.5f, yld-sft) in December off 6lb lower; not a bad run in listed novice handicap at Navan latest and this is much less demanding; seems best going right-handed so this track could suit; interesting.
runners.comment: Won off 6lb lower at Punchestown in Dec', solid fourth at Navan latest, this is easier

runners.name: Nettuno Italia
runners.spotlight: First reserve; won over hurdles at Punchestown (2m7f, heavy) in January; below best twice since, including on chase debut at Clonmel on latest; needs more off this mark.
runners.comment: Below best twice since January hurdles win, soundly beaten on chase debut latest; reserve

runners.name: Lisleigh Lad
runners.spotlight: Two-time chase winner; has mostly been running well this season, placed at Thurles (2m5f, yielding) and Leopardstown (21.5f, yielding); another decent run when fourth of 17 at Fairyhouse (2m5f, yld-sft) latest; place claims off 1lb lower.
runners.comment: In decent form this season, fourth of 17 at Fairyhouse latest, place shout off 1lb lower

runners.name: Petit King
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; winning hurdler is 0-8 over fences and yet to trouble the judge; pulled up and beaten 30l on last two chase starts; others hold more pressing claims.
runners.comment: 0-8 over fences and yet to trouble the judge, poor last twice, others preferred; reserve

runners.name: Clifftop
runners.spotlight: Won a beginners' chase over C&D (good) in August 2024; dual-purpose sort has failed to add to his tally since and he is best on good ground; fit from the Flat but needs the track to dry out.
runners.comment: C&D winner in Aug 2024, no joy since, fit from the Flat but needs the track to dry out
runners.owner: O B P Carroll & Ralph Kelly & Thomas Cox

runners.name: Killinure Lass
runners.spotlight: Five-time hurdles winner (including here) is 1-6 in chases, with that win also coming over this C&D (2m6f, gd-yld); clearly likes it here but lacks a run and remains 4lb above her last chase win.
runners.comment: Dual-course winner inc' over C&D, likes it here but lacks a run and 4lb above last win

runners.name: Midweek Voices
runners.spotlight: Has lost way since back to back wins in early 2025; pulled up and beaten 76l in last two chase starts; hard to fancy on recent showings.
runners.comment: Lost way since back to back wins in early 2025, pulled up latest, hard to fancy

runners.name: Tullyveery Lad
runners.spotlight: Gained a third chase win here over C&D in August off 6lb lower; sole run since a 5l third in a handicap hurdle here in October; however, overall record fresh suggests he might need this after a break.
runners.comment: Gained third chase win here in Aug, off since October, might need this after break
runners.owner: Pat Tennyson & N P Morrissey & Raymond Micheal Cody

runners.name: Rochestown
runners.spotlight: Five wins under Rules, with four coming at this venue; has won the last two renewals of this race, on good and gd-yld, but handles softer conditions; below best when last seen in December but goes well fresh and return to his favourite track could spark a revival; dangerous to dismiss.
runners.comment: Course specialist, has won the last two renewals, goes well fresh, dangerous to dismiss

runners.name: Ringdufferin
runners.spotlight: Two-time hurdles winner for Gordon Elliott is 0-7 over fences; in poor form when last seen in June 2025 and is now with a new yard; best watched for now.
runners.comment: Two-time hurdles winner, 0-7 over fences, off since June 2025, new yard, watch

runners.name: Carrig Sam
runners.spotlight: Both wins over hurdles at Fairyhouse in early 2022; no joy since and 0-7 over fences; off since February 2025 and likely best watched on first start for new yard.
runners.comment: Two wins over hurdles, 0-7 over fences, off since Feb' 2022, new yard, best watched

races.race_name: West Waterford Novice Handicap Chase

runners.name: Kotkito Bello
runners.spotlight: One of two for yard; dual hurdles winner rated 10lb higher in that sphere; sole chase start resulted in a 3.5l win in a beginners' at Thurles (18.5f, gd-yld) in October; pulled up in hot handicap hurdle at Fairyhouse latest but unexposed over fences and could go well in first handicap chase.
runners.comment: Won sole start over fences in Oct', rated 10lb higher over hurdles, interesting

runners.name: Kilbrin Rocco
runners.spotlight: 1-7 over fences, the win coming at Clonmel in January (2m, heavy); soundly beaten off revised mark twice since and handicapper slow to relent; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten off revised mark twice since Clonmel win, others appeal more

runners.name: In The Trenches
runners.spotlight: Long-standing maiden; has run well in defeat over fences and hurdles; heavy ground a valid excuse on latest and is 4lb lower than when a 9l runner up in a much stronger race than this at Cork (17.5f, good) in October; has run well here before and he's a player.
runners.comment: Long-standing maiden, fine run at Cork in Oct', ground excuse latest, has course form

runners.name: Torquay Road
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdles winner; sound effort on chase debut in a beginners' at Downpatrick (20.5f, gd-yld) when a 5l third but tailed off on softer ground at Fairyhouse (2m) on latest in January; soft ground a concern on handicap chase bow.
runners.comment: Solid third on chase debut, struggled on soft latest, ground a worry on h'cap chase bow

runners.name: A Snow White Park
runners.spotlight: Shock winner on Rules debut over hurdles at Ballinrobe (2m, good) last May; has failed to replicate that form in five runs since and is probably best watched on her chase debut.
runners.comment: Shock win on debut, not as good five starts since, best watched on chase debut

runners.name: Nas Na Riogh
runners.spotlight: Two-time winner over hurdles is 0-9 in chases; ran well when third at Galway (2m2f, soft) last August but out of form when last seen in November and needs to revive after a 141-day break.
runners.comment: 0-9 in chases, ran well at Galway last Aug' but lost form, off since Nov', revival needed

runners.name: Splashing Out
runners.spotlight: Hurdles and chase winner; out of sorts since winning at Down Royal in October but has slipped back down to her last winning mark and ran well on only previous visit to this track; can't dismiss.
runners.comment: Won off this mark at Down Royal in Oct, below best since but has gone well here before

runners.name: Soldante
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden hurdle here in November 2023 but no joy since; not disgraced on chase debut at Clonmel (2m, soft) last month but doesn't look obviously well treated for first handicap chase.
runners.comment: Maiden hurdle win here in Nov' 2023, not obviously well treated for first h'cap chase
runners.owner: P McCormack & David P McCormack & Sarah Glynn

races.race_name: SETU Students Day April 20th Handicap Chase

runners.name: La Tene
runners.spotlight: One of three for yard; took a step forward in a 2m3f handicap hurdle at Clonmel (soft) on latest, outpaced and rallying for a 7l third; drop back to this trip for her chase debut is not an obvious move on that evidence.
runners.comment: Outpaced before rallied well for third over 2m3f latest, drop in trip a query on chase bow
runners.owner: John Patrick Ryan & Miss Gillian Ryan

runners.name: Spanish Maid
runners.spotlight: Produced her best handicap run to date on stable debut in a Wexford chase (19.5f, soft) on latest when attracting market support; 3lb higher now but this shorter trip should suit on that evidence; chance.
runners.comment: Good effort at Wexford when well backed latest, up 3lb, drop in trip a plus, chance

runners.name: Courtbrack Boy
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the seventh attempt over hurdles at Clonmel in February; raised 7lb and came up short back there on latest, though the 2m3f trip may have stretched him; could go well dropped back in distance for his first handicap chase.
runners.comment: Won over hurdles at Clonmel in Feb', trip too far latest, may go well in first h'cap chase
runners.owner: Michael P Murray & Timothy R Murray

runners.name: Finnicky Filly
runners.spotlight: One of three for yard; lost form after winning at Clonmel in December (16.5f, heavy) but hinted at a revival back there latest when catching the attention of the stewards; didn't run badly off this mark on only previous chase start; not discounted.
runners.comment: Hinted at a revival at Clonmel latest, ran okay off this mark in sole previous chase

runners.name: Zolpharine
runners.spotlight: One of three for yard; 1-17 over hurdles and extended record to 0-21 over fences on latest at Clonmel (23.5f, sft-hvy) when never better than midfield; big drop in trip here; others make more appeal.
runners.comment: 0-21 over fences, never better than midfield on latest, others make more appeal

runners.name: Cerberus
runners.spotlight: Two wins over hurdles in 2019 but nothing since and his recent Cork fourth (2m1f, yielding) was a pretty remote one; 10lb higher mark in this sphere and others make more appeal.
runners.comment: On long losing run, recent Cork fourth over hurdles a remote one, 10lb higher over fences

runners.name: Itsalonglongroad
runners.spotlight: Veteran pulled up on first start for this yard at Clonmel in December but has a fine record here (four wins, three over fences) and is just 1lb higher than when winning over C&D last August; ground versatile and can't discount.
runners.comment: Four-time course winner (three chases), poor stable debut but can't dismiss at this track

runners.name: L'Amiral Fromentin
runners.spotlight: Modest form in points and similar story so far over fences and over hurdles under Rules; hard to make a case for on all known form.
runners.comment: Modest form in points and under Rules, hard to make a case for on form

runners.name: Halsafari
runners.spotlight: Veteran won in fine style over hurdles at Cork (2m, yld-sft) in November; however, he has a strike rate of 1-30 in chases and his last three runs in this sphere have not been encouraging.
runners.comment: Strike rate of 1-30 in chases and his last three runs in this sphere have been poor
runners.owner: John P Bowe & Michael J Bowe

runners.name: Set The Tone
runners.spotlight: 0-11 over hurdles and pulled up in sole start over fences at Thurles in February; similar outcome back over timber on latest; others preferred.
runners.comment: Maiden pulled up over hurdles and on chase debut last twice, others preferred

races.race_name: George Wood Years Of Service Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Hornica
runners.spotlight: Made the frame in big-field Cheltenham bumper (good ground) last April and showed fair form when placed in two soft-ground novice hurdles at start of this year; ideally needs to learn to settle better but has strong claims.
runners.comment: Showed fair form when placed on both hurdling starts; leading contender

runners.name: Yeld To Call
runners.spotlight: Tamely dropped out of contention in valuable series final at Kelso (2m, good to soft) last month but posted good effort when second at Taunton (2m, heavy) two starts ago and has possibilities if able to replicate that form on the quicker surface here.
runners.comment: Dropped out tamely last time but form of earlier Kelso second gives her a shout

runners.name: Lady Kara
runners.spotlight: Runner-up on hurdle debut at Hereford (2m, good) in October and both subsequent fourths (2m5f/2m) were respectable; reportedly had a breathing issue last time and now returns from break with a tongue-tie fitted; each-way claims at least.
runners.comment: In the frame in all three bumpers and all three hurdling starts; each-way claims at least

runners.name: Merely A Detail
runners.spotlight: Good-ground bumper winner who has made the frame on all four hurdling starts; managed only fourth of six on handicap debut at Fakenham (2m, soft) in December but the forecast faster ground here ought to suit better; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Only fourth of six on handicap debut but the faster ground here ought to suit better

runners.name: Willow Edge
runners.spotlight: Third foal from a fair 2m hurdle/chase winner; wears a hood on debut, which is somewhat unnerving.
runners.comment: Third foal from a fair 2m hurdle/chase winner; wears a hood on debut

runners.name: Lilycala
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; dam unplaced in bumper/2m hurdles, half-sister to useful jumper Yalltari; wears tongue-tie on debut; has fair standard to reach.
runners.comment: Out of half-sister to useful jumper Yalltari; has fair standard to reach on debut

runners.name: Pink Walls
runners.spotlight: Lowly rated Flat maiden; 250-1 for recent hurdling debut at Haydock, where a mid-race jumping error caused saddle to slip and he was pulled up.
runners.comment: Lowly rated Flat maiden; pulled up (saddle slipped) when 250-1 for hurdle debut

runners.name: Minnie Belle
runners.spotlight: Struggled on first two appearances, the latter over C&D three weeks ago.
runners.comment: Struggled on first two appearances, the latter over C&D three weeks ago

runners.name: After The Ball
runners.spotlight: Showed some promise in Irish points (albeit without winning) but was remote when fourth on last month's hurdling debut at Bangor (2m, good) and needs to take a good step forward here.
runners.comment: Remote when fourth on last month's hurdling debut; improvement required

runners.name: Miss Denver
runners.spotlight: Struggled on first two outings (bumper/hurdle) but this well-bred mare showed her first glimmer of promise when fourth, albeit well held, at Chepstow (2m, good to soft) last month, and ought to have more to offer.
runners.comment: Well-bred mare; first glimmer of promise when fourth at Chepstow last month

runners.name: Mega Etoile
runners.spotlight: Made fairly encouraging bumper debut on heavy ground in February but well held twice since, latterly when 80-1 for recent hurdle debut at Fontwell.
runners.comment: Well held when 80-1 for recent hurdle debut at Fontwell

runners.name: Bonnet's Secret
runners.spotlight: Last of 13 when making debut in Warwick bumper (good to soft) in November; can only be watched on this hurdling debut.
runners.comment: Last of 13 when making debut in Warwick bumper in November

runners.name: Liasse d'Argent
runners.spotlight: Well beaten when a big-priced outsider for debut at Hereford (2m, good) in November.
runners.comment: Well beaten when a big-priced outsider for debut at Hereford in November

races.race_name: Green 4 Motor Company 30th Anniversary Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Juggernaut
runners.spotlight: Course winner; resumed from four months off back hurdling with a very good second of seven in 2m2f Fontwell handicap 23 days ago; tries a much longer trip now after that strong-finishing effort and weighted to go well off a 1lb lower mark.
runners.comment: Course winner; very good Fontwell second latest; tries longer trip but weighted to go well

runners.name: Jewel Eyed Judy
runners.spotlight: Opened her account at Leicester (2m4f) in November but well below par subsequently, tried in cheekpieces when pulled up at Taunton (3m) 26 days ago; tongue-tie is added now.
runners.comment: Opened her account at Leicester (2m4f) in November but well below par subsequently

runners.name: Scudamore
runners.spotlight: Winless since scoring over 2m2f here in April 2023; failed to build on earlier promise when sixth of seven in 3m3f Sedgefield handicap last month but could bounce back in different headgear now.
runners.comment: Failed to build on earlier promise when Sedgefield sixth last month; could bounce back

runners.name: Seahouses
runners.spotlight: Scored at Market Rasen (2m7f) in November and posted another solid effort when third of six in another 2m7f handicap there last month; ought to be thereabouts eased 1lb.
runners.comment: Solid third at Market Rasen last month; ought to be thereabouts again eased 1lb

runners.name: Getaway With You
runners.spotlight: Bagged a fourth success of the season at Chepstow (2m3f) in February; seemed unsuited by dropping back to 2m when a solid fourth of ten at Hereford 13 days ago and not out of things over this more suitable distance.
runners.comment: Back to 2m when solid fourth at Hereford latest; not out of things over this longer trip

runners.name: East Eagle
runners.spotlight: Back to winning ways at Catterick (3m1f) in January and has continued in good form, second of eight in another 3m1f handicap there last time; in the mix once more.
runners.comment: Good second at Catterick last time; he's in the mix once more
runners.owner: Mrs Janet Cooper & M E Sowersby

runners.name: The Last Drop
runners.spotlight: Remains without a win and she came in a below-par fifth of nine in 3m1f handicap at Huntingdon last month; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Remains without a win; below-par fifth of nine in 3m1f handicap at Huntingdon latest

runners.name: Kampari
runners.spotlight: Seemed well suited by the longer trip when gaining a breakthrough success in 3m2f Warwick handicap hurdle 24 days ago; not taken lightly despite a 2lb weights rise.
runners.comment: Breakthrough success at Warwick 24 days ago; not taken lightly despite 2lb weights rise

runners.name: The Kings Soldier
runners.spotlight: Little to enthuse over in three maiden hurdles this season, beating only one at Market Rasen (2m4f) last month; has undergone breathing surgery ahead of his handicap debut.
runners.comment: Little to enthuse over in three maiden hurdles this season; undergone breathing surgery

runners.name: Floating Rock
runners.spotlight: Course winner in 2022; had hinted at promise this season until beating only one in 2m2f handicap here in October; returns after wind surgery now.
runners.comment: Hinted at promise this season until beating one here in October; returns after a wind op

races.race_name: Mid Warwickshire Cleaning Supplies Mares' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Granny Hawkins
runners.spotlight: Came good at Hereford in the autumn and showed even better form when following up there (also 2m, good) last month, after a break; moves up in grade here but might still be improving; must be considered.
runners.comment: Up in grade after her two Hereford wins but might still be improving

runners.name: Callin Baton Rouge
runners.spotlight: Four-time hurdles winner on good ground (once over C&D); ended 2025 with good second at Doncaster (2m, good) and made a satisfactory reappearance last month; can pose a significant threat from off the pace.
runners.comment: Four hurdles wins (one over C&D); made satisfactory return last month; high on the list

runners.name: D Day Arvalenreeva
runners.spotlight: Started off in handicap hurdles with three good-ground wins for Kevin Philippart de Foy last spring (2m-2m1f) and is back on a good mark now; just a respectable fourth when back over hurdles at Ludlow (2m, good) three weeks ago but still enters calculations.
runners.comment: Won three times on good ground last spring and is back on a good mark now

runners.name: Jaminska
runners.spotlight: Close third in Cheltenham Grade 2 novice (2m good to soft) in October and again ran commendably well when third in Newbury Listed race (2m, soft) a few weeks later but was tailed off when reappearing in a handicap last month and it's hard to know what to expect from her here.
runners.comment: Placed in Grade 2/Listed races in the autumn but tailed off in a handicap last month

runners.name: Fromheretoeternity
runners.spotlight: Slightly disappointing when made favourite at Hexham last month but this largely consistent mare has edged her way back down to a workable mark and probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: Shade disappointing last time but this largely consistent mare probably wont be far away

runners.name: Borna Gem
runners.spotlight: Made shaky start to hurdling career during the winter but benefited from combination of drop in trip and switch to good ground when winning 2m Ludlow maiden by 4l last month; probably needs to improve again to overcome opening handicap mark but that's possible.
runners.comment: Displayed improvement to win Ludlow maiden last month; more needed again on handicap debut

runners.name: Max Of Stars
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who enjoyed a resurgence last year, winning three times over hurdles and once on the Flat; absent since November, though, and will need to resume in peak form to have any chance off current mark.
runners.comment: Enjoyed resurgence last year (four wins) but returns from break on fairly tough mark
runners.owner: Mrs S D Pearson & Ollie Pears

runners.name: Anariza
runners.spotlight: 1-7 over hurdles; held her own in two good-quality novice races at start of this year and wasn't disgraced in valuable series final at Kelso last month; in calmer waters here but her tendency to finish weakly is a worry.
runners.comment: Has held her own in some good-quality races this year but tends to finish weakly

runners.name: La Marquise
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced since winning hurdle debut in October 2024 but has performed well on both outings this year, latterly when close second in small-field Plumpton handicap (2m2f, good to soft) last month; considered.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 6yo who was close second in small field at Plumpton last month; considered

runners.name: Mary
runners.spotlight: Returned to form with front-running win at Warwick (2m3f, good) in September but absent since tame finishing effort in November (breathing issue reported) and remains on fairly tough mark.
runners.comment: Made all at Warwick in September but absent since lesser effort in November

races.race_name: Racecourse Streams On Racing TV Extra Handicap Chase

runners.name: Felton Bellevue
runners.spotlight: None too consistent but he has won five times over fences, latest at Plumpton (3m1f, soft) in February off 3lb lower; beaten a neck back there off this mark last month and his best chance of further success is perhaps back at that venue.
runners.comment: None too consistent but he has won five times over fences, latest at Plumpton in February.
runners.owner: Mrs H Munn, R E Good, B Case

runners.name: Pescatorius
runners.spotlight: Won twice for Olly Murphy in the autumn and subsequently sold for 5,500gns; hasn't been in the same form in three runs for his current trainer; cheekpieces worn on his last three starts are dispensed with on this step up in trip; still has time on his side but all of a sudden has a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Won twice for Olly Murphy in the autumn but hasn't been in the same form since.

runners.name: Carpe Diem
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark over fences at Huntingdon (2m4f, soft) in February with first-time visor applied (retained since); in the money in two runs since and now steps up beyond 3m for the first time; holds place claims at least.
runners.comment: Got off the mark over fences at Huntingdon in February; in the money in two runs since.

runners.name: Unspeakable
runners.spotlight: Landed his second success over fences, at Hereford (3m1f, good) in October off 1lb lower mark; second at Plumpton the following month but was tailed off at Wincanton when last seen; has been freshened up since and holds claims if putting his best foot forward.
runners.comment: Won over fences at Hereford in October; second at Plumpton the following month.

runners.name: Yes And Yes
runners.spotlight: Sole success over fences came at Chepstow in March last year off 2lb higher; soundly beaten since and steps up to beyond 3m for the first time; down to a career-low mark and needs this extra test of stamina to bring out improvement.
runners.comment: Sole success over fences came at Chepstow in March last year; soundly beaten since.
runners.owner: David W Jagger & Mrs Belinda Clarke

runners.name: Illogical Logic
runners.spotlight: Landed his sole win off this mark at Newton Abbot (3m2f, good) last June but has been tailed off on his last two starts; would be considered if operating at his best but needs to sharpen up.
runners.comment: Sole success at Newton Abbot last June; has been tailed off on his last two starts.

runners.name: Chief Black Robe
runners.spotlight: Back with his former trainer on his last five starts; landed a four-timer for this yard in 2023; hasn't won since and has failed to complete on his last two starts; visor worn last time is left off; well handicapped if able to return to anywhere near his best form.
runners.comment: Landed a four-timer in the summer of 2023 but hasn't won since.

races.race_name: Bet At racingtv.com Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Renoir
runners.spotlight: Now 2lb lower than when winning at Hereford (2m, good) in spring 2025 but wasn't firing on all cylinders in the autumn when last seen and needs subsequent wind op to have made a difference; cheekpieces removed after one use.
runners.comment: Not firing on all cylinders in the autumn when last seen; had wind op in February

runners.name: Three Pikes
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner who was placed in three good-ground maiden hurdles (2m1f-2m2f) last summer; safely held in Ludlow handicap last month, after a break, but might have needed that outing and remains lightly raced.
runners.comment: Last month's handicap debut was underwhelming but that was his first run since September

runners.name: Juan Bermudez
runners.spotlight: Gained all three hurdle wins over C&D on good ground, the latest by 7l from the front last June; ran well to a point here last month, after a layoff, and will be dangerous off this mark if that outing brought him on.
runners.comment: Three-time C&D winner; ran well to a point last month, after a break; shortlisted

runners.name: Chief Sunday
runners.spotlight: Chasing didn't go to plan in the autumn (three attempts) and he's looked out of sorts over hurdles lately; will appreciate this ease in grade but still needs huge boost from the first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Out of sorts this spring; needs huge boost from first-time cheekpieces

runners.name: Time Interval
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D and at Warwick (2m) on good ground in May last year but his two runs in cheekpieces this spring were underwhelming and he needs to respond well to a first-time visor.
runners.comment: C&D winner last spring but needs to respond well to change of headgear today

runners.name: Klassy Pearl
runners.spotlight: 0-5 over hurdles but seemed to run well when second of four on handicap debut at Exeter (2m, heavy) in February and wasn't disgraced when midfield in valuable series final at Kelso (2m, good to soft) last month; sports first-time cheekpieces here; could have a say.
runners.comment: 0-5 over hurdles but not disgraced in valuable series final last month; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Chicago Storm
runners.spotlight: Out of form in December when last seen over hurdles but looked back in good nick when winning on AW in February and is 5lb lower than when scoring over C&D (good to soft) in March 2025; interesting.
runners.comment: Won on the AW in February and is 5lb lower than when scoring over C&D in March 2025

runners.name: Jet Of Dreams
runners.spotlight: Five-time chase winner who was placed on hurdle return at Kempton (2m, good) in October; absent since lesser effort in November but current mark is workable if he's fully tuned up.
runners.comment: Five-time chase winner; placed over hurdles in the autumn; returns from a break

runners.name: Legendary Day
runners.spotlight: Useful on the Flat in his prime but suffered heavy defeat in that sphere last month, after a layoff, and also struggled in a handicap hurdle three weeks ago; sports refitted cheekpieces; others have much less to prove.
runners.comment: Well beaten on both starts since a break (Flat/hurdle); sports refitted cheekpieces

runners.name: Farhh Echo
runners.spotlight: Showed significant improvement when fourth in Worcester maiden (2m, good) last summer but safely held in all four subsequent handicaps, the latest here (2m6f) last month; needs to turn a corner.
runners.comment: Safely held in all four handicaps, the latest here (2m6f) last month

runners.name: Estacas
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on old form for David Bridgwater but absent since two disappointing runs in November 2023; considered only if lively in the betting before this stable debut; had wind op in 2024.
runners.comment: Absent since two disappointing runs in November; market helpful on stable debut

runners.name: Ufouria
runners.spotlight: 0-7 over hurdles but kept on for respectable fourth at Bangor (2m, soft) in February, her fourth start for Alastair Ralph, and has dropped another 5lb since; may benefit from this move back up in trip; worth a second look.
runners.comment: 0-7 over hurdles but dropped 5lb since respectable fourth in February; worth second look

runners.name: Can U Feel It
runners.spotlight: Did not get seriously competitive in four 2m novice hurdles, although attracted the stewards' attention when making steady late headway on one occasion; up in trip for handicap debut; worth a glance in the betting.
runners.comment: Did not get seriously competitive in four 2m novice hurdles; handicap debut today

races.race_name: Geely Leamington Spa Novices' Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Bond Broker
runners.spotlight: Doesn't help himself by tending to race too freely but got off the mark over fences at fifth attempt, with front-running success at Ludlow (2m4f, good) last month; 5lb rise is offset by Jay Tidball's claim here, and this drop back in trip won't be a problem; respected.
runners.comment: Won from the front over 2m4f at Ludlow last month and will also be suited by this trip

runners.name: Park Annonciade
runners.spotlight: Started off for Sarah Humphrey with two disappointing hurdle runs in the autumn but has turned things around over fences since, and kept on well to win by 7l at Huntingdon (2m, good) a fortnight ago; recent 6lb rise demands more from him here, though.
runners.comment: Kept on well to score at Huntingdon this month but 6lb rise demands more from him here

runners.name: Pure Carbon
runners.spotlight: 2-6 over fences this season, the wins gained at Huntingdon (2m, good) and Musselburgh (2m, good to soft); gave another good account when close third of five at Ascot (2m1f, good) last month and should be fine on this left-handed track; good chance.
runners.comment: 2-6 over fences this season; comes here after good effort at Ascot last month; good chance

runners.name: Mister Cessna
runners.spotlight: Won readily by 5l on British chasing debut at Uttoxeter in October (2m, good); absent since disappointing run over 2m4f on New Year's Day but could still have potential and will be dangerous if back on song after 108-day break.
runners.comment: Off since disappointing run on New Year's Day but could still have potential over fences

races.race_name: Racing TV 'Grassroots' Open Hunters' Chase

runners.name: Killer Clown
runners.spotlight: Useful in his prime; drew clear to win Ludlow hunter chase (2m4f, good) by 13l last month and followed up in open point at Hornby Castle (2m6f, good to firm) eight days ago; obvious contender.
runners.comment: Drew clear to win Ludlow hunter chase last month and followed up in a recent point

runners.name: Largy Pearl
runners.spotlight: Honourable second to top hunter chaser Barton Snow over C&D (good) on rules debut last May and 3-3 in points this year; the one to beat.
runners.comment: Second to top hunter chaser Barton Snow over C&D last May and 3-3 in points this year

runners.name: Kansas Du Berlais
runners.spotlight: Made the frame (albeit safely held) in two good-quality hunter chases (2m/2m1f) last May and dug deep to beat useful rival in small-field open point at Charing (3m, good to soft; subsequently disqualified) in January; turned over at short odds in latest point but still enters calculations.
runners.comment: Dug deep to beat useful rival in small-field point in January; enters calculations

runners.name: Amidnightstar
runners.spotlight: Absent for three years after winning maiden hurdle in February 2023, and finished about 31l behind Killer Clown on last month's rules return at Ludlow (2m4f, good); close second in two-runner point since; tongue-tie added today.
runners.comment: About 31l behind Killer Clown at Ludlow last month; the one with most to prove

runners.name: Important Notice
runners.spotlight: Creditable third at Cheltenham (3m2f, good) on rules debut just under a year ago and comes here after two small-field open point wins at Trebudannon (both 3m, good to soft) this spring; has a part to play.
runners.comment: Placed at Cheltenham last year and comes here after two small-field point wins this spring

races.race_name: Satsuki Sho (Japanese 2000 Guineas) (Grade 1) (Colts & Fillies) (Turf)
Racing For Charity Hcp (C5) (Handicap) (All Weather)

runners.name: Meepmeep
runners.spotlight: Just 1-30, though often placed as when beaten a head by Hailtothevictors on dirt (1m) this month; a furlong shorter may suit him better, but still frame material in this field.
runners.comment: Just 1-30; a furlong shorter may suit him better, but still frame material in this field

runners.name: Firefoot
runners.spotlight: Just 1-27 but has made the frame in three of his four starts on dirt as when fourth over C&D 18 days ago; drops to Class 5 for the first time; high on list.
runners.comment: 1-27 but in the frame in three of his four starts on dirt; first start down in Class 5

runners.name: Rosewood Fleetfoot
runners.spotlight: Placed once in 12 starts on turf; drawn widest on this switch to dirt and has plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Placed once in 12 starts on turf; drawn widest on switch to dirt and has plenty to prove
runners.owner: Lam Ka Lai & Yeung Wing Shut

runners.name: All Eyes On Me
runners.spotlight: Has twice made the frame on dirt (1m) this year, but well beaten either side and now 0-17; opposable from his wide draw.
runners.comment: Has twice made the frame on dirt this year, but well beaten either side and now 0-17
runners.owner: Enrico Chow Yue Hin & Jennifer Ng Mau Yee

runners.name: Viva Taste
runners.spotlight: 0-9; best effort when runner-up on his reappearance over C&D in October, but hasn't quite matched that form since; drop to Class 5 a positive even though he isn't alone in that respect.
runners.comment: 0-9; has run well over C&D and drops to Class 5 for the first time
runners.owner: Jimmy Chung Kwok Man & Dominic Chung Won Lok

runners.name: All Are Mine
runners.spotlight: 1-25 in Hong Kong, the win coming in a Class 4 over C&D at the end of 2023; mixed form on turf lately and drawn wide, but Zac Purton regaining the ride is a positive.
runners.comment: 1-25 in Hong Kong, the win coming over C&D at the end of 2023; Zac Purton regains the ride

runners.name: Foremost Teddy
runners.spotlight: Won two Class 5s on dirt (1m/1m1f) in December, but has struggled at Class 4 level since; however, much respected back in this grade with his ability to handle the conditions not in doubt.
runners.comment: Struggled at Class 4 after winning two Class 5s on dirt; much respected back in this grade
runners.owner: Tse Ming Yee & Emily Tse Wing Man

runners.name: Conspiracy Witness
runners.spotlight: 0-15 including finishing out of the frame in both starts on dirt; no improvement for the addition of cheekpieces last time and a high draw is of little help either.
runners.comment: 0-15 including finishing out of the frame in both starts on dirt; drawn wide
runners.owner: Arthur Antonio Da Silva, Betty Da Silva & Teresa Da Silva

runners.name: Golden Fairy
runners.spotlight: Reserve; often a front-runner; 3lb lower than when winning on turf here (1m2f) last June, but modest since and will need to improve on his two previous dirt efforts.
runners.comment: Modest since winning on turf here last June; held in two previous dirt efforts
runners.owner: Rex Wong Siu Han, Ivan Wong Siu Kei & Ann Wong Pui Gee

runners.name: Happydearhappydeer
runners.spotlight: Placed in three consecutive starts on dirt (1m/1m1f) the winter before last, but hasn't shown much in four starts since returning from a year off in January and is now 0-12; others preferred.
runners.comment: Hasn't shown much in four starts since returning from a year off in January and now 0-12
runners.owner: Hong Yiu, Lynn Fu Lam Wu Et Al

runners.name: Hailtothevictors
runners.spotlight: Made it 13th-time lucky when beating Meepmeep by a head on dirt (1m) 18 days ago; up 6lb but remains in the same grade and has run well over this far before; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Up 6lb for his narrow win here last time, but remains in the same grade; shortlisted
runners.owner: Graham Kot Li Heng & Amy Chau

runners.name: Mega Force
runners.spotlight: 1-7 in Ireland and unplaced in all 16 local starts; takes another drop in class, but even so his claims aren't compelling.
runners.comment: Unplaced in all 16 local starts; takes another drop in class, but claims aren't compelling

runners.name: Spangle Fortune
runners.spotlight: 2-35 in Hong Kong with the wins coming over 6f/1m on dirt, but his last two efforts back at Class 5 level leave plenty to be desired.
runners.comment: 2-35 in Hong Kong; last two effort back at Class 5 level leave plenty to be desired

runners.comment: Well beaten all four turf starts and can't be recommended on this dirt debut
runners.owner: Chan Wing Kwan, Chan Wai Leung & Chadwick Mok Cham Hung

runners.name: Never Too Soon
runners.spotlight: Struggled to get seriously involved from stall 11 over C&D 18 days ago, but made the frame on consecutive Class 3 turf starts (6f/7f) beforehand and has good claims if judged on those efforts.
runners.comment: Struggled from wide stall here last time but twice made the frame in Class 3 beforehand

runners.name: Armour War Eagle
runners.spotlight: Began this year with string of good efforts on turf/dirt and had double-digit draws when below that form on last two outings; benefits from combination of inside stall and drop in grade today; good chance.
runners.comment: Began this year with string of good turf/dirt runs; eased in grade today; good chance

runners.name: Happy Shooter
runners.spotlight: Reserve; below form when last in action (raced too freely) but had been in good nick on turf beforehand; will be making his dirt debut if he gets a run today.
runners.comment: Largely in good form on turf this year; this will be his dirt debut
runners.owner: 23/24 Danny Shum Chap Shing Trainer Syndicate

runners.name: Good Luck Babe
runners.spotlight: Reserve; now 5lb lower than when winning on turf (7f) in October but has not been firing on all cylinders in recent months.
runners.comment: Won on turf in October but not firing on all cylinders in recent months

runners.name: Igot
runners.spotlight: Won 7.5f maiden in Australia in 2024 but was a down-the-field outsider on first three Hong Kong starts (6f-7f); dropped in grade but drawn wide for dirt debut; not the most obvious answer.
runners.comment: A down-the-field outsider on first three HK starts; dropped in grade for dirt debut
runners.owner: Wong Kam Choi & Benson Wang Ding Ben

runners.name: Giant Leap
runners.spotlight: Kept on for second on turf two starts ago and should be fine back on dirt, but finished last when last in action and has become hard to predict.
runners.comment: Second on turf two runs ago but well beaten since; has become hard to predict

runners.name: Everstar
runners.spotlight: Badly out of form on turf in recent weeks and not yet proven on dirt; swaps usual cheekpieces for first-time eyeshields.
runners.comment: Badly out of form on turf in recent weeks and not yet proven on dirt; too risky
runners.owner: Jackie F C Hang, Colin L P Cheung & Fredrick F C Lun

races.race_name: Lifting Every Young Life Hcp (C3) (Handicap) (All Weather)

runners.name: Pi Legend
runners.spotlight: Came good in Hong Kong at sixth attempt, making all over C&D from stall 12 this month; 7lb higher in a higher-grade race today but not drawn so wide this time and further progress is possible.
runners.comment: Up in grade after making all over C&D 18 days ago but further progress is possible

runners.name: Igor Stravinsky
runners.spotlight: Exploited combination of dip in grade and favourable draw when scoring by a neck over C&D 18 days ago; evidently in good nick but has more on his plate on this quick return to Class 3.
runners.comment: Won narrowly from favourable draw over C&D this month; quickly back up in grade
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Cyrus Li Kin Wai

runners.name: Lady's Choice
runners.spotlight: Now 1lb lower than when going close on turf on seasonal debut in October, but his subsequent form has been underwhelming and he's not yet proven on dirt.
runners.comment: Generally disappointing since going close on seasonal debut, and not yet proven on dirt
runners.owner: HK Association Of Lady Horse Lovers Syndicate

runners.name: Must Go
runners.spotlight: Well suited by dirt (five-time C&D winner) and lurks on a dangerous mark but has been in poor form lately; leap of faith required to support him.
runners.comment: Five-time C&D winner; lurks on a dangerous mark but has been in poor form lately

runners.name: Blazing Wind
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner this season; made the frame over C&D on last two appearances, despite not enjoying the run of the race on either occasion, and is reunited with Zac Purton today; must be considered.
runners.comment: Two C&D wins this season; still in good form and has Zac Purton back on board

runners.name: Mr Energia
runners.spotlight: Won readily over C&D in October, his third dirt success, but ended 2025 with two very disappointing runs (1m/6f) and was again soundly beaten when back in action this month; has a point to prove.
runners.comment: Won over C&D in October but three lesser efforts followed; has a point to prove

runners.name: Power Koepp
runners.spotlight: Twice made the frame on turf last month and didn't run badly when sixth at Happy Valley recently; on a tempting mark now but this 8yo is not easy to predict on just his third dirt start (latest was in March 2023).
runners.comment: Twice in the frame on turf last month but not easy to predict on this rare dirt start
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Simon Ng Kar Ming

runners.name: Beauty Glory
runners.spotlight: Versatile regarding trip/surface, with latest win gained over 1m on turf, but has been placed only once from six starts since returned to this grade; others look stronger.
runners.comment: Placed only one from six starts since returned to this grade; others look stronger

runners.name: Aurora Patch
runners.spotlight: Scored over C&D in February and probably bettered that performance when close second from stall 10 over C&D last month; nudged up 2lb to another career-high mark but has better draw today; respected.
runners.comment: Went close from stall 10 over C&D this month; respected from more favourable berth

runners.name: Romantic Son
runners.spotlight: In excellent form on turf this season (three wins) and continued the good work when third over C&D on recent dirt debut; still unexposed on this surface and has good 10lb claimer booked today but wide draw a negative.
runners.comment: Triple turf winner this season; placed over C&D on recent dirt debut; drawn wide today

runners.name: Tin Fook
runners.spotlight: 0-8 on turf in Hong Kong but makes stable/dirt debut from a workable mark, has the plum inside draw and has finished second in two recent C&D trials; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Makes stable/dirt debut from a workable mark and has the inside draw; shortlisted

races.race_name: Helping Older Adults Age Well Hcp (C3) (Handicap) (Turf)

runners.name: Everyone's Star
runners.spotlight: Placed three times over C&D this season; managed only a midfield finish here on latest outing six weeks ago but that came from stall 12 and a subsequent 2lb drop helps; no surprise if he features.
runners.comment: Placed three times over C&D this season and current mark is workable; a possible

runners.name: Aerovolanic
runners.spotlight: Improving 4yo who bids to complete a C&D hat-trick after two convincing wins, the latter in this grade four weeks ago; latest 9lb rise asks a question of him but he needs to be taken very seriously.
runners.comment: Improving 4yo who bids to complete a C&D hat-trick after two convincing wins
runners.owner: Gwenny Wu Pui Kwan & Daniel Yeung Ngai

runners.name: Uranus Star
runners.spotlight: Gained his Hong Kong win over 1m but coped well with drop back in trip when second to an exciting prospect over C&D three weeks ago and has obvious claims if in similar form again.
runners.comment: Second to an exciting prospect over C&D last month; player if in similar form again

runners.name: Wonderstar
runners.spotlight: Ran fairly well over 5f here a fortnight ago and continues to slip down the weights but is yet to convince stamina-wise over 7f.
runners.comment: Ran fairly well over 5f here a fortnight ago but is yet to convince stamina-wise over 7f

runners.name: Sky Jewellery
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who looked very promising in early part of 2025, with latest of three wins gained in very good style over 1m; could still have a lot more to offer but may not be ideally suited by the drop back in trip on this first start for just over a year.
runners.comment: Made excellent progress in early part of last year; returns from 378-day absence

runners.name: M M Concord
runners.spotlight: Bettered inauspicious Hong Kong debut when midfield over C&D last month, but didn't look straightforward that day and remains best watched.
runners.comment: Second HK run was much better than the first but he remains best watched

runners.name: Perfectday
runners.spotlight: Dual 6f winner; still quite lightly raced after only seven starts but recent efforts suggest he needs this step up in trip to unlock further improvement.
runners.comment: Dual 6f winner; needs this new trip to unlock further improvement
runners.owner: Choi Yui Hin & Daniel Choi Yui Fai

runners.name: Chill Easy
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish 4yo; not disgraced on any of his three Hong Kong starts (7f-1m) but needs a boost from the first-time cheekpieces today.
runners.comment: Not disgraced on first three Hong Kong starts but needs boost from new cheekpieces today

runners.name: Golden Champ
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner over 6f/7f in New Zealand but launched Hong Kong career with two low-key runs (6f/7f) and may need more time to acclimatise.
runners.comment: Won four times in NZ but has made a low-key start to his Hong Kong career

runners.name: Exceed The Limit
runners.spotlight: Unable to land a telling blow when fifth over 6f on first two Hong Kong starts, but his New Zealand form suggests this longer trip will suit better; not ruled out each-way, despite wide draw.
runners.comment: Fifth over 6f on first two HK starts; NZ form suggests this longer trip will suit better

runners.name: Make You Smile
runners.spotlight: Group 3 winner as a 2yo in Britain but, following a long absence, made just a mildly encouraging Hong Kong debut over C&D last month; drawn on the outside today; needs that recent outing to have brought him on.
runners.comment: Made just a mildly encouraging HK debut over C&D last month and has wide draw today
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Tony Tang Wai Bun And Vicky Tang

runners.name: Nucleozor
runners.spotlight: 3-3 when based in New Zealand (including Listed win) but his four runs in Hong Kong for Danny Shum last season were underwhelming and this stable debut comes after a 315-day absence.
runners.comment: Safely held on all four Hong Kong starts; makes stable debut after 315-day absence

races.race_name: Enabling Professionals To Flourish For Their Charge Handicap (C3) (Turf)

runners.name: Victor The Rapid
runners.spotlight: Reserve; scored in Class 4 at Happy Valley in February and has held his form well since but will be moving up in grade if he gets a run today; needs to find something extra.
runners.comment: Has held his form since a win in February but moves up in grade today

runners.name: Sunny Da Best
runners.spotlight: Kept on well to make the frame over 7f twice last month and continues to edge down the weights; this drop back in trip won't be a problem; each-way claims at least.
runners.comment: Kept on well to make the frame over 7f twice last month and 6f also suits; each-way claims

runners.name: Super Express
runners.spotlight: Runner-up three consecutive times over 7f since winning over that trip in February and might still be making steady progress, but has tough draw for this first run in a sprint.
runners.comment: Progressive over 7f this year but has tough drawn for this first run in a sprint

runners.name: Anode
runners.spotlight: Made the frame on consecutive 7f starts here in October but recent C&D form is uninspiring and he's now 0-12 in Hong Kong.
runners.comment: Recent C&D form is uninspiring and he's now 0-12 in Hong Kong

runners.name: South Star
runners.spotlight: Much better known as a 7f/1m performer; did well to make the frame from stall 10 over C&D on last month's sprint debut but has the same awkward draw today.
runners.comment: Made the frame over C&D last month, his first sprint, but has another awkward draw

runners.name: Ka Ying Attack
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mainly over 7f this season, but ran a big race in defeat over C&D when dropped back in trip last month and is better drawn today; one to consider.
runners.comment: Ran big race in defeat over C&D when dropped back in trip last month; better drawn today

runners.name: Robot Lucky Star
runners.spotlight: Won quite comfortably over 5f here in October, his second start, and was twice a close second over 6f at Happy Valley in November; upped in grade for this first run since but probably still has potential.
runners.comment: Won over 5f in October and was second twice over 6f in November; still lightly raced

runners.name: Cornerstone
runners.spotlight: Safely held over C&D on all three Hong Kong starts and eligible for a drop in grade now.
runners.comment: Safely held over C&D on all three Hong Kong starts and eligible for a drop in grade now
runners.owner: 24/25 Dennis C H Yip Trainer Syndicate

runners.name: Vulcanus
runners.spotlight: Class 4 winner on dirt in January but he's been finding it a struggle at this level; favourable draw not enough to make him of interest.
runners.comment: Class 4 winner on dirt in January but he's been finding it a struggle at this level

runners.name: Thousand Spirit
runners.spotlight: Off the mark in Hong Kong at 11th attempt, gamely making all over C&D a fortnight ago; 6lb higher in higher-grade race today but can't grumble about his draw.
runners.comment: Gamely made all over C&D to open HK account last month; up 6lb in higher-grade race today

runners.name: Gold Patch
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 3yo who did well to win over C&D last month, given he was hampered and encountered traffic, and is now 2-3; moves up in grade today but should have plenty more to offer; major player.
runners.comment: Overcame interference and some traffic to win last month and is now 2-3; big player

runners.name: Magnemite
runners.spotlight: Built upon promising debut when winning 7f maiden in Australia in April 2025 but this Hong Kong debut comes after a one-year absence; probably best watched.
runners.comment: 7f maiden winner in Australia; makes Hong Kong debut after one-year absence

runners.name: Sovereign Fund
runners.spotlight: Reserve; returned to form with Happy Valley win on stable debut in February but could not land a telling blow off new mark on either start since.
runners.comment: Won on stable debut in February but safely held off today's mark twice since
runners.owner: Glenn Chan Wai Cheung & Raymond Chan Ka Shun

runners.name: Chicken Dinner
runners.spotlight: Won over this trip in New Zealand in 2024 but made a low-key Hong Kong debut here (5f) in November and has not raced since.
runners.comment: Absent since low-key Hong Kong debut over 5f here in November

races.race_name: Fostering An Enabling And Inclusive Society Hcp (C2) (Handicap) (Turf)

runners.name: Seraph Gabriel
runners.spotlight: Lost out only by a nose in 1m2f Royal Ascot handicap last year and has performed well on two of his first four local starts, more recently when fifth (just behind Patch Of Cosmo) in last month's Hong Kong Derby (1m2f); 10lb better off with that rival today; interesting.
runners.comment: Fifth in last month's Hong Kong Derby and still unexposed locally; interesting
runners.owner: Marc Chan & Lily Chan Li Lei

runners.name: Stunning Peach
runners.spotlight: Predictably found Group 2 too hot here (1m) a fortnight ago, but looked in excellent form when winning his last two handicaps (1m/1m1f), and is of interest if judged on those exploits.
runners.comment: Found Group 2 too hot last time but won his last two handicaps; respected

runners.name: Ka Ying Generation
runners.spotlight: Has mixed record this season but was placed at big odds in 1m2f Group 2 in November and seemed to respond well to refitted cheekpieces when rallying third over C&D six weeks ago; could have a say.
runners.comment: Responded well to refitted cheekpieces when rallying third over C&D last month; a possible

runners.name: Nautical Force
runners.spotlight: 2-7 when based in Ireland, signing off with small-field win over today's trip last August; looks an interesting recruit to the Hong Kong scene but is 5lb out of the weights for this local debut.
runners.comment: Useful when based in Ireland but 5lb out of the weights for this Hong Kong debut

runners.name: Winning Wing
runners.spotlight: Progressive 5yo who has collected three 1m2f handicap wins here this season and ran about as well as could have been expected when third of six in Group 1 over that trip last month; respected.
runners.comment: Triple 1m2f winner this season; ran well in Group 1 over that trip last month; respected

runners.name: Sky Heart
runners.spotlight: Won over 7f here on seasonal debut in October and has largely remained in good form since; beaten under 1l when third over 1m here last month and will be fine over today's trip; probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: Won over 7f here on seasonal debut in October and has largely remained in good form since

runners.name: Voyage Samurai
runners.spotlight: Has five course wins (7f-1m) on his CV, but this season has been a washout so far and today's trip may stretch him.
runners.comment: This season has been a washout so far and today's trip may stretch him

runners.name: Joy Of Spring
runners.spotlight: Late-maturing 6yo who scored over this trip at Happy Valley in December and probably posted another career-best performance when close second here (1m) last month; no surprise if he's on the premises again.
runners.comment: Late-maturing 6yo who went close over 1m here last month; can feature again
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Edward Hung & Doris Hung

runners.name: Sword Point
runners.spotlight: Smart in his prime but has regressed this season; soundly beaten on his last three starts (1m-1m1f) and needs to find a way to stop the rot.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten on his last three starts and needs to find a way to stop the rot

runners.name: Patch Of Cosmo
runners.spotlight: Improving 4yo who was placed over C&D in Classic Cup before keeping on for commendable fourth in last month's Hong Kong Derby (1m2f); back in a handicap today; could easily have more to offer.
runners.comment: Back in a handicap after commendable fourth in last month's Hong Kong Derby

runners.name: Awesome Fluke
runners.spotlight: Dual winner at Happy Valley (1m1f/1m) this season; too inconsistent to rely upon heavily but looked unlucky not to finish closer than fourth here (1m; denied clear run) last month and may yet have a bigger run in him.
runners.comment: Dual winner this season; not very consistent but may yet have a bigger run in him

runners.name: Gentlemen Legacy
runners.spotlight: Bids to complete a hat-trick after two narrow 1m2f course wins; latest 6lb rise asks quite a lot of him but he's evidently in prime form.
runners.comment: Bids to complete a hat-trick after two narrow 1m2f course wins

runners.name: New Forest
runners.spotlight: Front-runner; in excellent form on dirt this season, winning three times, but struggled when back on turf for last three starts and remains on a daunting mark on this surface.
runners.comment: In excellent form on dirt this season but has struggled since returned to turf

runners.name: Moments In Time
runners.spotlight: Excellent third in C&D Group handicap in November but has lost his way since, albeit last month's 7f defeat is not viewed harshly; others have much less to prove.
runners.comment: Excellent third in C&D Group handicap in November but has lost his way since

races.race_name: Oaklawn Handicap (Grade 2) (Dirt)

runners.name: Sovereignty
runners.spotlight: Improved throughout 3yo season in 2025 and crowned US Horse of the Year; winner of Grade 1 Kentucky Derby at Churchill Downs in May (beat Journalism by one and a half lengths) and three times at Saratoga afterwards, notably Grade 1 Belmont Stakes in June (beat Journalism by 3l) and Grade 1 Travers Stakes in August (most impressively by 10l); missed Breeders' Cup Classic with raised temperature; better suited by 1m2f than 1m1f; first start for eight months and must give weight all round on Oaklawn debut.
runners.comment: US Horse of the Year in 2025 when won three 1m2f Grade 1s; gives weight to all on comeback

runners.name: Journalism
runners.spotlight: Won three Grade 1s in 2025, including Preakness Stakes at Pimlico (1m1.5f) in May and Haskell Stakes at Monmouth Park (1m1f) in July; good efforts when in frame in similar events afterwards, on final start just under 4l fourth in Breeders' Cup Classic at Del Mar; second over 1m2f to Sovereignty in Kentucky Derby (beaten just over 1l) and Belmont Stakes (beaten 3l) but now 4lb better off and reverts to best trip; sound claims to reverse that form on Oaklawn debut.
runners.comment: Won three Grade 1s around 1m1f; twice beaten by Sovereignty at 1m2f but now 4lb better off
runners.owner: Bridlewood Farm, Don Alberto Stable Et Al

runners.name: Duke Of Duval
runners.spotlight: Progressive at lower level in 2025, winner of maiden and allowance race at Ellis Park and another allowance race here (1m) last December; unable to make an impact in two Grade 3s this year, on latest start beaten 6l when fifth to Skippylongstocking in Essex Handicap here; needs to spring a massive surprise.
runners.comment: Progressive at lower level last year but well held in two Grade 3s since; very stiff task

runners.name: Liberal Arts
runners.spotlight: Very useful form at slightly shorter than this 1m1f (has won five times, notably one Grade 3) but nothing to suggest he can run with this class of rival; best form when chasing good pace; sold $120,000 since latest start.
runners.comment: Very useful form at slightly shorter than this but he's likely to be outclassed

runners.name: Publisher
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden after his first 12 starts (beaten over 30l by Sovereignty in 2025 Kentucky Derby) but seems improved as 4yo; winner of all three starts over 8.5f here in 2026, namely maiden, allowance and black-type stakes, the latter won by just over 1l, staying on strongly; needs to improve markedly to trouble these, but at least he's going the right way and should be suited by 1m1f.
runners.comment: Improved in 2026, winning three times here over 8.5f including black-type race; stiff task
runners.owner: Gus King & The Estate Of Brereton C Jones

runners.name: White Abarrio
runners.spotlight: High-class form at best, winning four Grade 1s including 2023 Breeders' Cup Classic at Santa Anita and 2025 Pegasus World Cup (1m1f) at Gulfstream Park; not quite so good last four starts, though shaped as if in need of race (clear 1f out, tired) when second to stablemate Skippylongstocking in 2026 Pegasus World Cup in January; can race up with the pace; Oaklawn debut.
runners.comment: Winner of four Grade 1s, but again not at his best when 2nd in Grade 1 Pegasus in January
runners.owner: C2 Racing Stable LLC, Gary Barber & La Milagrosa Stable LLC

races.race_name: American Stakes (Grade 3) (Turf)

runners.name: Twirling Point
runners.owner: Tracy D Henline & Paula P Henline

runners.name: Almendares
runners.owner: CYBT, McLean Racing Stables Et Al

runners.name: Genius Jimmy
runners.owner: Pappas Horse Racing Corp, Colin Gala Et Al

runners.name: King Of Gosford
runners.owner: Benowitz Family Trust, CYBT Et Al

runners.name: Seal Team
runners.owner: Perry R Bass II & Ramona S Bass

runners.name: Sumter
runners.owner: Perry R Bass II & Ramona S Bass

races.race_name: American Stakes (Grade 3) (Turf)

runners.name: Twirling Point
runners.owner: Tracy D Henline & Paula P Henline

runners.name: Almendares
runners.owner: CYBT, McLean Racing Stables Et Al

runners.name: Genius Jimmy
runners.owner: Pappas Horse Racing Corp, Colin Gala Et Al

runners.name: King Of Gosford
runners.owner: Benowitz Family Trust, CYBT Et Al

runners.name: Seal Team
runners.owner: Perry R Bass II & Ramona S Bass

runners.name: Sumter
runners.owner: Perry R Bass II & Ramona S Bass

runners.owner: C2 Racing Stable LLC, Gary Barber & La Milagrosa Stable LLC

races.race_name: Satsuki Sho (Japanese 2000 Guineas) (Grade 1) (Colts & Fillies) (Turf)
Tote Guarantee, Never Beaten By SP Alleged Stakes (Group 3)

runners.name: Beset
runners.spotlight: Ended last season on a high with 1m4f Listed win at Naas in a race in which stablemate Beset floped; maintained progression at Naas on return; more needed now.
runners.comment: Ended last season on a high with 1m4f Listed win, maintained progression at Naas on return
runners.owner: K Blake/Mrs E Palmer/M Wilson/N Luck

runners.name: Twain
runners.spotlight: Unbeaten in two races at two; going was heavy for Group 1 Saint-Cloud win from stablemate Mount Kilimanjaro; missed all of last season; a fascinating runner on the comeback trail now.
runners.comment: Unbeaten in two races at two, going was heavy for Group 1 Saint-Cloud win, unraced in 2025
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Trustyourinstinct
runners.spotlight: Good form early last season, notably recording back-to-back wins at this venue in a Listed 1m4f contest and Group 3 International Stakes over this trip; below best when attempting a repeat of his 2024 Group 3 win at Leopardstown's Irish Champions meeting; well beaten at the same venue on final start.
runners.comment: Good early-season form at the track in 2025, well below best in two starts in the autumn

runners.name: Royal Rhyme
runners.spotlight: Won the Brigadier Gerard Stakes on his return in 2024; later, generally found things too demanding at the highest level (sixth in the International, seventh of eight in the Irish Champion) before finishing with a career-best in taking third behind Anmaat in the Champion Stakes; ran only twice last season (both Group 1s); pick of this field on best form; effective on soft.
runners.comment: Ran only twice last year, the pick of the field on his Group 1 form, effective on soft
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Green Impact
runners.spotlight: Beat the top-class Delacroix in Group 2 at Leopardstown at two (1m, good); sixth in 2,000 Guineas before making all in Listed race at Leopardstown last June (1m1f, good); respectable sixth in the Irish Derby (1m4f, good); front-running effort petered out when fourth in York Group 2 event, though was best of the Classic generation; should make a smart middle distance 4yo.
runners.comment: Group 2 winner at two, Listed scorer at three, should make a smart middle distance 4yo

runners.name: Starford
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner, progressive in handicaps last season; unplaced in a race won by stablemate Beset on his only stakes race attempt; plenty to prove as a result.
runners.comment: Three-time winner, unplaced in a race won by Beset on his only stakes race attempt

runners.name: Carolina Jetstream
runners.spotlight: Boasts useful form at Dundalk, the scene of all four wins; limited turf experience; major improvement needed to make an impact at this level.
runners.comment: Boasts useful form at Dundalk, major improvement needed to make an impact at this level
runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar

runners.name: Red Letter
runners.spotlight: Fourth in Group 1 Moyglare Stakes; came good after being beaten by the smart Barnavara at Navan last June; handled soft when landing 1m Listed race at Killarney; left an impression she would stay further with 1m1f course win last August; turned the tables on Barnavara then, but that rival prevailed again here in September; should be in the mix.
runners.comment: Showed a consistent standard of form at a high level last season, should be in the mix

runners.name: Eastwatch
runners.spotlight: Two wins over extended 1m1f at Gowran last year (maiden/conditions race); failed to make an impact at Listed level next two starts over further; runner-up in Irish Lincoln on return; unplaced favourite for a 1m2f handicap last time; up against it at this level.
runners.comment: Runner-up in Irish Lincoln on penultimate start, weak form in Listed races, up against it

runners.name: Bubble Gum
runners.spotlight: Finished midfield in French and German Oaks in 2024; Listed winner at Cork for Paddy Twomey 12 months ago; below that form when fifth in Munster Oaks last June; never showed with a chance over 1m here on stable/seasonal debut; unlikely to feature
runners.comment: Never showed with a chance over 1m here on stable/seasonal debut, unlikely to feature

runners.name: Omni Man
runners.spotlight: Ended last year with a Listed AW third; one of three runners for the stable; will need to raise his game to feature here
runners.comment: Ended last year with a Listed AW third, will need to raise his game to feature here

runners.name: Edward Hamilton
runners.spotlight: Frankel colt made a belated winning debut in an ordinary Dundalk AW maiden last September when allowed to dictate; never showed with a chance in a Listed race at Leopardstown; tried with blinkers; stable's second string, best watched.
runners.comment: Dundalk maiden winner, no threat in a Listed race on only other star, yard's second string
